programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A medical student is murdered - and Dr Sloan suspects it is only a matter of time before the killer strikes again. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The medic investigates the suspicious deaths of several homeless people, who are seemingly being killed for their organs at a clinic funded by a friend. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An unpopular woman is accused of killing her wealthy husband - but luckily for her, she has a twin sister to provide her with a seemingly airtight alibi. Robert Vaughn guest stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The hi-tech problem-solvers are caught in a dangerous situation when they try to rescue a group of scientists trapped inside a crumbling biodome designed for use on Mars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The day of Toby and Happy's wedding arrives, but when the team is called to help extinguish a catastrophic tunnel fire, it seems their colleagues might not make it to the ceremony

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The hi-tech problem-solvers are caught in a dangerous situation when they try to rescue a group of scientists trapped inside a crumbling biodome designed for use on Mars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The day of Toby and Happy's wedding arrives, but when the team is called to help extinguish a catastrophic tunnel fire, it seems their colleagues might not make it to the ceremony

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby becomes personally involved in the case of a murdered naval scientist who she regards as a kindred spirit. The team's initial inquiries turn up little concrete information about the victim's life, but the forensic specialist makes a breakthrough when she decodes some of the mysterious markings covering the body. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team members are surprised when Gibbs asks DiNozzo's father to join them in their investigation of a murder committed at the US Naval Academy. However, Tony Sr's experience turns out to play a vital role in the inquiry, which finds the team members hunting for a missing nuclear weapon they suspect is about to be sold on the black market. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ziva confronts her past when the team is ordered to protect her father, Mossad director Eli David, at a gathering of former heads of NCIS. The agents discover he is being targeted by terrorists who have hijacked a shipment of advanced weapons from the army. Drama, guest starring TJ Ramini and Michael Nouri, with Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. The safe house is attacked, leaving Vance in hospital and Eli missing, with his bodyguard Amit Hadar dead. The movements of all the Palestinians are accounted for at the time of the attack, making it unclear who was responsible, and Ziva suspects her Mossad replacement Liat is hiding something. Drama, starring Michael Nouri, Cote de Pablo, Rocky Carroll and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. One of their few leads is a dead turkey Tony and McGee find in the man's fridge, so they take it to Ducky for an autopsy. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.desc: The hi-tech problem-solvers are caught in a dangerous situation when they try to rescue a group of scientists trapped inside a crumbling biodome designed for use on Mars

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison dreams a bank robbery has been carried out by her brother Michael - who surprises her by arriving in town shortly afterwards, and soon gets involved with a conwoman. Supernatural drama, starring Patricia Arquette and Ryan Hurst

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Harm finds himself trapped below deck with a sailor accused of murder and his guard. To make matters worse, the ship is sinking and the only escape route is blocked. Drama, starring David James Elliott

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: A Hawaiian lifeguard joins the team, but he finds the busy beach very different to his normally laid-back way of living

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A visiting doctor is suspected of murdering one of his colleagues in an alcoholic stupor. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A photographic lab erroneously sends Dr Sloan someone else's photos, embroiling him in the death of an escort girl. Meanwhile, Amanda prepares to give birth. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A sinister psychiatrist offers to help Dr Sloan exact revenge on a downsizing expert who threatens the medic's job - in return for a deadly favour. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is left stranded on a deserted island when the plane crashes on the way to Toby and Happy's honeymoon

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Three weeks after becoming stranded on a deserted island, tensions run high among the agents, and in-fighting threatens their chances of being rescued

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby visits a pharmaceutical lab to investigate a murder case, but when armed men storm the facility and hold everyone hostage, she is trapped within the building with no means of communicating with the outside world. Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The notorious Tri-State Sniper appears to have claimed another victim when a petty officer is shot dead in a crowded park. However, when the team discovers the killer in question was already in custody on the day of the shooting, it becomes clear a copy-cat is on the loose. As their investigation progresses, the agents learn the victim was recently found guilty of placing a camera in the female changing rooms of the USS Camden, earning himself plenty of enemies - but did any of them hate him enough to kill?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to investigate the murder of a retired US Navy lieutenant commander, and the inquiry forces Ducky to reveal he has been working as part of a secret society dedicated to solving cold cases. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo is reunited with his old flame, Jeanne Benoit, when insurgents in Sudan attack a group of volunteer doctors, including her husband. Travelling to the crime scene to conduct a joint murder-kidnapping investigation, Dinozzi and McGee offer their assistance in searching for the missing doctors. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray, with Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Kyle Friedgen is accused of killing his ex-girlfriend, but while his case is dismissed due to a mishandled warrant, his life and reputation are marred by suspicion that he got away with murder. Determined to clear his name in full, Friedgen volunteers to be court-martialled, on the condition that the NCIS team conducts its own investigation. Meanwhile, Bishop and Jake discuss their marital problems. Naval crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham, with Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is left stranded on a deserted island when the plane crashes on the way to Toby and Happy's honeymoon

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Three weeks after becoming stranded on a deserted island, tensions run high among the agents, and in-fighting threatens their chances of being rescued

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team uncovers an alliance between al-Qaeda and a Mexican drug cartel when it investigates a shoot-out at a pawn shop. As the agents explore the reasons behind the unusual coalition, a Mexican police chief with a vendetta against the Mendoza gang joins them

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Hetty enlists the help of a former colleague when an ex-Navy intelligence officer is murdered, but things go awry when her old friend threatens to kill her. The team races to save Hetty and expose her one-time workmate as having lied to the victim in order to make money from a seemingly charitable venture

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a dishonourably discharged marine is accused of beating a Japanese tourist to death, having already been linked with the rape of a businessman's daughter. He claims that he is in love with the girl he is alleged to have raped, and that the accusations being thrown in his direction are a fabrication initiated by her father

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a naval seaman and an infamous arms dealer are found dead near the Mexican border. Evidence suggests the men were trying to smuggle a wooden box into the US, but there is no sign of the container, nor any clue as to what could be inside. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam heads to Sudan to help a CIA agent gather evidence of al-Qaeda terrorist training, but Callen is forced to rush to the country too, when one of them is killed while carrying out the mission. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison's investigation into a series of apparently random killings puts her family in danger, and Bridgette is asked to compile a class presentation on her mother's job. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette and Maria Lark

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Part one of two. Admiral Chegwidden sends Harm and Bud to Australia to represent a US Navy officer standing trial for murder. The accused admits he was the killer, and compounds the felony by claiming he then stole the dead man's identity - but Lt Cmdr Brumby is waiting in the wings to ensure he pays for the crime. Military legal drama, starring David James Elliott and John M Jackson

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The two Marine brothers of a sailor battling leukaemia are killed in the line of duty, and to spare the servicemen's family further grief, the secretary of the US Navy seeks the NCIS team's help in locating a suitable bone marrow donor. Meanwhile, Bishop returns home to Oklahoma for Thanksgiving. Military crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Deeks places his job in danger when he shoots and kills an unarmed man during a mission to retrieve Semtex explosives from a marine warehouse, prompting unexpected emotions for Kensi as she contemplates losing her partner. Crime drama, starring Eric Christian Olsen and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are investigated by the FBI on suspicion of murdering arms dealer La Grenouille. programme.desc: A government weapons truck is hijacked as part of a plan to allow a dangerous criminal to escape from jail. programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Unable to put the tragic loss of her baby behind her, a heartbroken Beth calls off the wedding, while the Scorpions decide to pose for a nude calendar to raise funds for the club's floodlights. Meanwhile, new parents Susan and Steve eagerly await Elaine's departure so they can be on their own with their baby _ little suspecting the houseguest has a surprise in store. Rural drama, starring Kerrie Taylor, Heather Peace, Daniel Ryan and Clare Holman

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Beth and Tom prepare to tie the knot, but reckon without a few last-minute hitches as Joe suddenly decides he can't be the best man and the bride worries over whether to invite her father. Meanwhile, Sally bites off more than she can chew, the green-eyed monster in Anna makes an appearance when Beth's cousin takes a shine to David and a multiple sclerosis sufferer requires assistance

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Dennis Waterman and former Corrie actress Georgia Taylor guest star in this special festive edition of the popular rural drama. A retired mountaineer (Waterman) still grieving over his son's death in a climbing accident gets a pleasant surprise when his student daughter (Taylor) returns for the holidays. But an even bigger shock is yet to come - not only is she pregnant, but the father-to-be is the very man her dad blames for her brother's demise. Meanwhile, the local women grow fed up with their menfolk when they arrive back from a booze run the worse for drink, prompting them to hang up their aprons and hand over all the Christmas organisation to the fellas. Beth doesn't know what to think when a patient predicts the new year will bring her and Tom the joy they have longed for, and romance is in the air for David and Anna. Lesley Dunlop, Philip Middlemiss, Kerrie Taylor and Samantha Giles star

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: David and Anna have a secret romance, Beth starts to feel paranoid about the amount of time Tom is spending at the factory, and Nathan and Kate both have important decisions to make about their futures. Starring Philip Middlemiss, Lesley Dunlop, Kerrie Taylor, Julian Lewis Jones, Adam Paul Harvey and Jenny Platt

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Learning Napoleon has returned from exile, Sharpe leaves Lucille to look after their farm and returns to service on the Prince of Orange's staff. It isn't long before he's reunited with the remaining Chosen Men for one of the most momentous military engagements of all time - the Battle of Waterloo. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and Cecile Paoli star

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads find themselves in a tight spot after a job turns to disaster in Russia. Penniless and desperate to go home, they visit the British Embassy, where news filters through of a department that allows men to travel the world fixing up British Government property. Oz's criminal past comes back to haunt them and threatens to ruin their chances, but, thankfully, Neville has friends in high places who save the day

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: The lads prepare to start work on the Ambassador's residence in Havana, but get off to a bad start with their new gaffer, who makes it clear he considers them a bunch of cowboys. Wyman catches the eye of a sultry local beauty during a night out, while Oz is captivated by the charms of the Cuban National Ballet's leading lady - and turns to Barry for advice on romance. Tim Healy, Noel Clark, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Feeling somewhat less than merry, Compo, Clegg and Foggy visit a friend in hospital before opening their presents. Meanwhile, Sid, Ivy, Nora and Wally Batty, have their relatives over to dinner. Christmas special from 1978, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie uncovers disturbing information regarding his ancestral line. Tyler's surprising return from university adds to his sense of worry, convincing him something serious is about to happen. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bryan welcomes his mail-order bride into his life - and his caravan - but there isn't enough room for her family as well. Llewellyn's student helpers disappear, so he hatches a plan to appropriate Boycie's staff and the new arrivals in one fell swoop. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie discovers Marlene has been discussing their marital problems with a counsellor, and the subsequent row results in him being thrown out of the house. With the hotel booked up and his car in a pond, Boycie's only option is to sleep on Bryan's caravan floor. Comedy, starring John Challis and Sue Holderness

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Return of the detective drama. Mike and his team are drawn into a world of Victorian dress-up and escapism when they investigate a murder at a Brokenwood steampunk event

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest encounters a man who intends to solve his father's murder a year earlier with the use of his brand-new invention - a time machine. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: World-weary detective inspector John Rebus goes on the trail of a copycat serial killer, only to be taken off the case pending an inquiry into the death of a convicted murderer he helped put behind bars 15 years earlier. Crime drama based on the books by best-selling author Ian Rankin, starring John Hannah, Jim Norton, Ewan Stewart, Sara Stewart and Joanna Roth

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jardine and Reid investigate the murder of a crook who masterminded the theft of a fortune in bullion 15 years previously - half of which was never recovered. Discovering several of his associates have also been bumped off, they deduce someone must be systematically killing the entire gang in a bid to get hold of the remaining loot. Starring James Macpherson and Blythe Duff

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A party of pilgrims is imprisoned within Shrewsbury Abbey on suspicion of murdering an old man whose body is discovered in a sack. Sleuthing monk Cadfael is soon on the scent of the killer, boiling the corpse down to its bones to ascertain the cause of death - and finding a tiny cross that could provide a vital clue. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Michael Culver, Julian Firth and Anthony Green

Liz May Brice and Mark Moraghan guest star

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nikki and Roger encounter a young lad who has been beaten and discover his family are under pressure from a loan shark seeking to recoup a vast sum of money. Further investigations reveal the boy's estranged father has been involved in a drug dealers' turf war and has disappeared without trace. When a body is found, it becomes clear the frightened youngster knows more than he's letting on

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Lorna Doone
programme.desc: Lavish adaptation of RD Blackmore's novel of lovers, outlaws and warring families in 17th-century Exmoor. Amelia Warner stars as the eponymous heroine, with Queer as Folk's Aidan Gillen as her evil aristocratic cousin, who wants her for himself at any cost. The cast also includes Richard Coyle, Martin Clunes, Barbara Flynn and Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Round Tower
programme.desc: Feature-length drama set in the 1950s, starring Emilia Fox as the rebellious teenage daughter of a successful businessman and his devoted wife, whose disenchantment with her privileged upbringing ultimately provokes a scandal in their small town. Keith Barron and Jan Harvey also star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A woman expecting her third child realises she cannot afford to care for her baby, so makes the painful decision to give it away to her wealthy but childless cousin. Shelagh befriends the woman in the next hospital bed and Dr Turner confides in Timothy about his worst fears. Sister Julienne looks for a new recruit to join the midwives, and the community prepares to celebrate the opening of a new park

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A man arrives in an East Anglian village with his sister Joanna to convalesce following a flying accident - but the residents' warm welcome isn't all it seems. Joan Hickson stars as the elderly sleuth, with Andrew Bicknell and Sabina Franklyn

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The body of a woman is found on train tracks in London, badly damaged from electrical burns, and the post-mortem stirs up troubling memories from Nikki's childhood

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: When he learns the identity of the new owner, Hugh tries to stop the farm sale, Matt is torn between his feelings for Charlie and April, and Meryl uncovers council corruption

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh's world is turned upside down after Harriet gives birth to their baby, while Meryl and Ajax clash over his plans for a cemetery on the family farm

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: Feature-length drama in which the Middle Ages' answer to Sherlock Holmes sets out to investigate a slave girl's disappearance and locate a treasured relic missing from his abbey. Unfortunately, no sooner has he begun searching for a connection than a dead body turns up on his doorstep, plunging him into a mire of murder and subterfuge. Starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Anthony Green, George Irving and Louise Delamere

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Terry and Max when they find themselves stuck in a traffic jam and are helpless to intervene as carjackers drag a woman from her vehicle, stabbing her fatally. The atmosphere deteriorates as the detectives work on the case, which involves a large-scale international gang, and Sam has to separate them when they come to blows

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The day of the interviews for the consultancy role arrives, and Sahira's judgement is tested when a patient whose condition she had previously diagnosed as a benign tumour is readmitted with serious complications. Meanwhile, Malick is forced to suppress his aggressive personality while dealing with a man suffering from a stab wound, and Dan books a weekend away in Barcelona for himself and Chrissie - but his grand gesture misfires

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Selena returns to the hospital, and is annoyed to learn Maggie is no longer a consultant - and an associate specialist position has been advertised. Ellen tells Nina to move back home with Abs, and gives the pair a present to thank them for their support, while Kelsey tells her latest date she is an air stewardess

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sally and Beth discover a man found badly beaten had argued with his runaway daughter Lucy the night before. Beth develops a soft spot for the girl, but can't get much out of her about the incident and her touchingly protective boyfriend Mark (guest star Joe Absolom) denies suggestions he might be involved. The family clearly have a lot to hide and Sally warns Beth not to get personally involved

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: An adventure for Billy turns into a nightmare for Carol. Pauline is on the warpath with Tony. Peggy reaches the end of a long battle but her plans seem insensitive to Kathy

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie secures a job interview at deals on wheels, Grant returns from Fiji and Billy is a prime witness in a court trial

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Tony is hauled over the coals for smuggling rifles into Britain, and Fitzpatrick blows his top on the eve of McCleod's funeral, angry that disillusioned widow Angela is not allowing it to be done with full military honours

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James, Siegfried and Tristan find different ways of dealing with their domestic problems, and Mrs Hall makes a surprise announcement. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer is asked to sell a sinister-looking mirror from a ballet school, and determines to finally marry Lady Jane. However, the object of his affections has some shocking news for him that suggests he may have left matters too late. Roger Lloyd Pack (Trigger in Only Fools and Horses) guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides the community needs an old folks' home, and takes her fight to the local council. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Harvey invites local students into Bayview to interact with the residents, and bribes one to deliberately annoy troublemaking resident Diana in the hope she will retaliate and be thrown out of the home

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley is almost ready to unveil his latest creation, but his odd behavour makes the trio want to find out what lies within his shed sooner than he would prefer. Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde, Bill Owen and Gordon Wharmby star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is stunned to catch Nora Batty in a church hall closet with Billy Chiselhurst. Realising he has a rival for her affections, he challenges Billy to a duel at dawn. Bill Owen, Kathy Staff and John Jardine star

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: When a body washes ashore, Jane is convinced it is a botched sea burial, but Davey believes otherwise. With a meal at stake, both are determined to prove each other wrong

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: A gunman begins murdering uniformed police officers, prompting the detective to taunt the killer on TV in the hope of inspiring him to turn his anger on him and come out into the open. To his horror, Luther also learns that Alice Morgan has been investigating his past - and is threatening to tell Zoe what she has discovered. Thriller, starring Idris Elba, Indira Varma, Steven Mackintosh and Saskia Reeves, with Ruth Wilson and Sean Pertwee. Written by Neil Cross (Spooks)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team is given the task of re-examining the unsolved case of peace protester Josh Livesey, found dead near a nuclear base in 1984. Other campaigners pleaded innocence at the time, but refused to co-operate with police, blaming the death on Secret Service skulduggery. Detective drama, starring Alun Armstrong, James Bolam, Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki travels to Mexico to attend the funeral of Luisa, a former intern at the Lyell Centre who was murdered in a carjacking in her native country. She soon stumbles across the post-mortem report and realises that the body is not that of her ex-colleague. Assisted by Dr Eva Vasquez and fired up with the idea that Luisa might still be alive, Nikki and Jack trace her last-known movements and are led to a farm where a cartel massacre was carried out. Crime drama, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A woman's corpse is discovered in a ploughed field, and the only lead is her husband, a potter. A year earlier, he suddenly decided to start a new life as a monk, suggesting that his wife may have committed suicide because she could not bear to lose him to the brethren - or that her spouse may have had a hand in her death. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This episode's contestants are a father and daughter from Glasgow, two sisters from London, and a couple from Belfast. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade takes Faith for a night out to cheer her up, but ends up having her drink spiked. She is saved from being attacked, but begins to fear her deafness makes her vulnerable. Ethan realises he needs to come clean to Fenisha after hearing a patient's story of becoming estranged from her daughter. Jacob is enraged by a note left by Tina, and demands she be more professional

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose receives footage of Steve leaving Faith's house at dawn. While she realises she has lost an ally, she decides he still has his uses as an instrument of her revenge - and soon after, he disappears without trace. Mike receives news from Professor Rhys that Osian's tumour is now completely inoperable, and resigns himself to the fact that his son is going to die. Osian does not give up so easily however, and is determined to go through with the experimental treatment regardless

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the most controversial moves in Premier League history

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Match of the Day Live

Burnley prevailed 2-1 when the sides met at Turf Moor in December thanks to goals by Ashley Barnes and Chris Wood and they will be keen to complete a double over Wolves given they could still be dragged into a relegation battle should they endure a poor end to the campaign. With analysis from Alex Scott and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in the Nidderdale Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in North Yorkshire, helping prepare for the return of both animals and visitors to the hills. She joins a team keeping track of adders emerging from hibernation to find out why numbers are falling. On his Cotswold farm, Adam Henson's struggling with the unpredictable spring weather, and Tom Heap's investigates how a rise in pet ownership is fuelling dog thefts. Plus, wildlife film-maker Jack Perks celebrates the grayling

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit some of Britain's most sumptuous and unusual locations, attracting large crowds intent on finding out what their hidden treasures are worth

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie befriends a patient during a delivery, but is troubled by the news that she has subsequently been readmitted to the Lady Emily after collapsing unexpectedly at home. Sister Frances decides she needs to know more of the outside world if she is truly to understand her patients' needs, and Cyril and Fred come to the aid of a family who have been evicted from their home - although Violet is less than happy about their acts of charity

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: As AC-12 struggle to deal with the repercussions of tragic events, Hastings makes one final bid to uncover institutionalised corruption before his time runs out. Crime drama, starring Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Leeds United v Manchester United at Elland Road, as Leeds looked to avenge a heavy 6-2 defeat in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Aston Villa v West Bromwich Albion, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Brighton & Hove Albion and Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations team using their special off-road vehicle

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Leominster in Herefordshire, where she learns about the history of the local hat and glove industries. Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri are helping the teams

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's a big day for Valerie, but Karen has forgotten something important. A documentary crew visits the prison, and Daniel helps an inmate struggling to find a way out of the system

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards returns with the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps an artist to find a period property in Hampshire, and joins a tree-planting initiative to celebrate the 10th anniversary of the South Downs National Park

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, hoping to breathe new life into a leaking tent, a scandi style coffee table and a miniature greenhouse.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by a candidate for the London mayoral election.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a team of roving reporters present topical stories from around the UK

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rainie and Stuart weigh up the pros and cons of Bernie being a surrogate, Zack offers to help Nancy get a job and the Carters throw a surprise picnic for Shirley, where Linda overhears her daughter calling Tamwar. Billy meets Janet's agent Estelle, who takes an shine to him and invites him for a drink. Sue Holderness (Only Fools and Horses' Marlene) joins the cast

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A party of German guests comes to stay and Basil resolves to ensure everyone is on their best behaviour. Regrettably, a nasty case of concussion sustained during a fire drill ensures his plans come crashing down. Starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: First episode of a new sitcom, co-written by and starring Rose Matafeo. A drunken New Year's eve hook-up becomes far more complicated for Jessie when she discovers her one night stand is actually a film star. What she thought would become an amusing anecdote soon turns into something else

programme.title: Dreaming Whilst Black
programme.desc: One-off drama about a young aspiring filmmaker trapped in his recruitment job. When he bumps into an old friend from film school who is now working at a production company, he gets the chance that he has been waiting for. Unfortunately, she fixes him a once-in-a-lifetime pitching opportunity which clashes with a career-saving presentation - forcing him to choose between going all in or giving up his dream entirely. Co-written by and starring Adjani Salmon

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Camilla and Noah are joined by Richie Driss from Blue Peter and comedians Jarred Christmas, Marlon Davis and Jenny Bede

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team comes to the aid of nine-year-old Maddie, who has mild cerebral palsy and needs a space of her own where she can start focusing on her sporty future

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The launch of a new eco-competition

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: It is time for a non-seasonal Christmas special, but things go awry when Amelia and her friends accidentally put Santa in a critical condition

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Someone is sabotaging this episode for their own personal gain, and Amelia must find out who before the whole show goes down the televisual toilet

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall challenges a cheetah to a race and meets a cute but deadly bird

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Historian Bettany Hughes investigates the ideas of ancient philosophers, starting with the Indian nobleman Siddhartha Gautama, more popularly known as Buddha. Thought to have been lived and tought between the sixth and fourth centuries BC, the sage and holy man inspired a diverse belief system that influences the lives of millions of people to this day. She travels to India, where the Buddha experienced the challenging ideas and extreme methods of wandering 'truth seekers', after he had abandoned his family and homeland in the Himalayas to embark on his philosophical quest to find a solution to human suffering

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Neil Robertson v Jack Lisowski in the second round, and the second session of John Higgins v Mark Williams

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Coverage of the annual event, held this year at St Jakobshalle in Basel, Switzerland, featuring day one of the apparatus finals

programme.title: Live Women's Six Nations
programme.desc: England v France or Ireland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Coverage of the final, as Pool A winners England take on the team that finished top of Pool B at a venue to be confirmed. The English were comfortable victors over Scotland and Italy in the pool stage, but will expect a tougher test this afternoon

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Barry Hawkins v Kyren Wilson, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby

programme.title: Saved and Remade
programme.desc: Presenter Sabrina Grant brings in a box of her own precious items for a transformation, a collection of denim clothing that belonged to her sister, who died suddenly 10 years ago

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. This time, the seven remaining contestants are challenged to make a chain collar and a sweetheart brooch, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of John Higgins v Mark Williams in round two, and the start of Shaun Murphy v Yan Bingtao. Three-time champion Williams recorded a 13-11 victory over 2015 title-winner Stuart Bingham in round two last year

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at the year that arguably marked the peak of new British pop as Wham!, ABC and Culture Club all made appearances. Dexys Midnight Runners' new Irish sound topped the summer charts for four weeks with Come on Eileen and their follow-up, Jackie Wilson Says, led to a notorious moment in TOTP history. It was also the year that Shalamar's Jeffrey Daniel introduced the moonwalk to British TV

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Tupac Shakur's image is known around the world as he was one of hip-hop's most photographed figures. But could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we know? This edition throws a lens onto an extraordinary life by focusing on 10 defining pictures, from famous shots to private snaps, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the hip-hop star best

programme.title: Hip Hop at the BBC
programme.desc: Hip-hop performances through the decades from the BBC archives, including the Sugarhill Gang in 1979 and Run DMC, LL Cool J and Eric B & Rakim in the '80s. Plus, Ice T, Monie Love, Fugees and the Roots in the '90s and latterly Dr Dre featuring Eminem, Dizzee Rascal and Jay-Z

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Featuring Adam Ant, Yazoo, Wham!, ABC and the Associates, all breakthrough acts in a golden year for British pop. Madness provide a little two-step with their ska revival, and Junior and Patrice Rushen's R'n'B tracks pack a punch. Pop-infused reggae beats are provided by Culture Club and Musical Youth, while the mods get a nod from the Jam and there's a performance by Tight Fit

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the concluding session of John Higgins v Mark Williams

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the second-round matches on the eighth day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Finding Jack Charlton
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a profile of the World Cup-winning footballer, looking at his special relationship with Ireland after becoming the national team's manager, his complicated relationship with brother Bobby, and his final battle with dementia. including contributions from his wife Pat and son John, alongside celebrated Irish players, musicians, writers and politicians, including Larry Mullen, Roddy Doyle, Brendan O'Carroll, Paul McGrath, Niall Quinn, Mick McCarthy, David O'Leary and Andy Townsend

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don takes viewers through the next steps of chilli seed growth. Joe Swift gets design inspiration from a courtyard garden, and Nick Bailey finds out about an annoying pest spoiling box hedges

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher goes wild in the Hambleden Valley in Buckinghamshire, on a whistle-stop tour of a chocolate-box landscape that has been the backdrop for many a TV drama and film. He also finds out how the old cottage industry of lace-making is poised for a comeback, and saddles up to meet a farming couple opening up their farm to enable safer riding in this corner of the countryside. Adam Henson is busy with some new arrivals and, as picking season dawns, Charlotte Smith investigates whether UK farmers can cover a shortfall in the number of seasonal farm workers needed to gather their harvests

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits a spectacular hill fort in Aberdeenshire, discovering the lost landscape of the Picts. Anne Lundon finds out why the Flow Country blanket bog is bidding for global recognition as a World Heritage Site and also meets renowned photographer Shahbaz Majeed in his favourite landscape - Glencoe. Euan Mcilwraith discovers the story of the Scottish cattle rancher, while Arlene Stuart marks the centenary of the birth of much-admired landscape artist Joan Eardley

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter shows how to grow potatoes in recycled containers, while Calum Clunie shares secrets of award-winning potato growing at his Leven allotment. George Anderson is at home in sunny Joppa and shows the progress of his speedy seeds for small spaces, and the programme catches up with Lucy Dalglish, whose modest Maryhill balcony is overflowing with edible promise this year

programme.title: Gymnastics
programme.desc: A round-up of yesterday's action from St Jakobshalle in Basel, Switzerland

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre, featuring Stuart Bingham v Jamie Jones and David Gilbert v Judd Trump

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new shows

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley visits Bridgewater Gardens in Salford to celebrate spring, and Gemma Hunt joins a Christian couple bringing joy to families through cooking

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Lisa Snowdon presents an appeal on behalf of Meningitis Now, a charity funding research, raising awareness and supporting families who have experienced the disease

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring two matches from the second round - Shaun Murphy v Yan Bingtao, and Mark Allen v Mark Selby

programme.title: Gymnastics
programme.desc: Action from St Jakobshalle in Basel, Switzerland, where the likes of Max Whitlock and Claudia Fragapane were representing a strong British team

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the penultimate sessions of two matches from the second round - Stuart Bingham v Jamie Jones and David Gilbert v Judd Trump

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Scandinavia, meeting climate change activist Greta Thunberg in Stockholm to find out how the young generation is determined to turn things around. He also learns how the Danish capital Copenhagen has pledged to be carbon neutral by 2025, and has a tour of a Gothenburg house installed with a hydrogen fuel system that could play a massive part in a carbon-free future

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: An exploration of how native creatures have adapted to survive in the human environment. In Melbourne, tree-dwelling possums have taken to sharing trees in city parks, risking encounters with traffic and cats. In Brisbane's parks, over the past 150 years of so, eastern water dragons have become twice the size of their wild cousins and without trees to climb their limbs are getting longer and claws shorter. Beyond the cities, dingoes eek out an existence in the inhospitable environment of a desert goldmine, while climate change and deforestation is posing a threat to koalas. Narrated by Barry Humphries

programme.title: Dangerous Liaisons (1988)
programme.desc: A philandering aristocrat in 18th-century France schemes with his manipulative former lover to seduce a virtuous young woman. As their conspiracy becomes ever more tangled, events take an unexpected turn. Stephen Frears' Oscar-winning drama of sexual politics, based on Pierre Choderlos de Laclos' novel, starring John Malkovich, Glenn Close, Michelle Pfeiffer, Swoosie Kurtz, Uma Thurman and Keanu Reeves

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day nine in Sheffield, featuring two second-round matches - Stuart Bingham v Jamie Jones and David Gilbert v Judd Trump

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights from today's second-round matches on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Noah enlists Matt to help him defeat Samuel, and sets a cunning trap - using Vanessa as bait. Hiro and Ando take extreme measures to break Suresh out of the mental institution where he is being held, and Peter's mother urges him to stay away from Emma, warning that she is destined to be complicit in thousands of deaths

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro's life hangs in the balance as his brain tumour worsens, and he begins to experience hallucinations in which many of his past decisions are re-enacted. Meanwhile, Sylar visits Claire in search of answers, and Samuel sets out to impress his long-lost love Vanessa - with terrifying consequences

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Louis feels trapped as Vicky's blackmailing escalates, and he wonders how much longer he can keep his double life a secret. Max grows more determined to catch the hospital thief, a desperate Dom campaigns for Ange to assist with his operation and Kian leads Lucky on a merry dance across the hospital as he tries to dodge a drugs test

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs, combining a mix of exciting new talent and returning contestants, compete for the North East, Yorkshire and Humber region with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. After their unique creations have been judged, one of them must leave the competition

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Plans are made to improve the mental health of the meerkats and wolverines, a koala has a nasty eye infection, and the flamingos show off their unusual nest building skills

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day 10 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of two second-round matches - Stuart Bingham v Jamie Jones, and David Gilbert v Judd Trump. At this stage last year, Martin Gould took on Kyren Wilson, and Barry Hawkins tested Neil Robertson's mettle. Fans also witnessed a gripping showdown between Anthony McGill and Jamie Clarke, as well as a clash between Ding Junhui and Ronnie O'Sullivan. It remains to be seen who will go through to the next stage in the 2021 tournament, but naturally all players are keeping their eyes on the prize

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1930s Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo sets out on another railway adventure as he explores Britain between the world wars. He begins with a tour of the Home Counties and beyond, starting in the city of Oxford, where in 1921 women were awarded degrees for the first time. At Somerville College, Michael finds out about pioneering crystallographer Dorothy Hodgkin, the only British woman to win a Nobel Prize in chemistry

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day 10 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of two second-round matches - Shaun Murphy v Yan Bingtao, and Mark Allen v Mark Selby. Three-time champion Selby was pushed to the limit by Murphy's first-round conqueror Noppon Saengkham at this stage last year, eventually prevailing 13-12, but was eliminated in the semi-finals when he was on the wrong side of a deciding frame against Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions for the last time as six contestants take to the famous black chair in the final of the quiz, with specialist subjects of Shackleton's Antarctic expedition of 1914-17, Cole Porter, the Vienna Secession, Jimmy Carter, Flanders and Swann, and the films of David Cronenberg

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to the Anantara Al Jabal Al Akhdar in Oman, which at 2,000 metres above sea level is the highest 5-star hotel in the Middle East and is a resort seeking to blend local tradition with modern tastes. Led by the hotel's mountain guru Maher, Giles and Monica undertake a test-run of the adventure course before it opens to guests, facing 30-metre drops and Giles's profound fear of heights

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Only Connect presenter Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quizan extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Josh Widdicombe and author Emma Dabiri joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Action from the evening session on day 10 in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of the second round

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of second-round matches at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, which were played over the best of 25 frames

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher goes wild in the Hambleden Valley in Buckinghamshire, on a whistle-stop tour of a chocolate-box landscape that has been the backdrop for many a TV drama and film. He also finds out how the old cottage industry of lace-making is poised for a comeback, and saddles up to meet a farming couple opening up their farm to enable safer riding in this corner of the countryside. Adam Henson is busy with some new arrivals and, as picking season dawns, Charlotte Smith investigates whether UK farmers can cover a shortfall in the number of seasonal farm workers needed to gather their harvests

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Tupac Shakur's image is known around the world as he was one of hip-hop's most photographed figures. But could just a handful of photos uncover new truths about someone we think we know? This edition throws a lens onto an extraordinary life by focusing on 10 defining pictures, from famous shots to private snaps, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the hip-hop star best

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Newby Hall, near Ripon in North Yorkshire, Treasures include a letter sent by Roald Dahl to a primary school, a Chippendale chair worth a king's ransom and football memorabilia harking back to the early days of the beautiful game - when a week's wages at Arsenal were less than the price of a cup of coffee today

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: Life in the treacherous seas that surround the South American wilderness. The 4,000-mile coast stretches from the cold waters of Cape Horn - where rockhopper penguins overcome huge challenges to raise their young - to the far north's much warmer climate, where huge elephant seals battle for position in the heat of the desert. Orcas ram-raid the beaches, grabbing seal pups to feed their young, and fishermen are also incentivised to gather the sea's bounty. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the concluding session of John Higgins v Mark Williams and the start of Shaun Murphy v Yan Bingtao. The biggest shock of round two last year was unseeded Norwegian Kurt Maflin's 13-11 victory over Scotland's four-time champion Higgins

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: Salvo and Livia have decided to spend some time apart, leaving the inspector gloomy and confused. When 60 security boxes are stolen from a small local bank, Montalbano must seek advice from Stella Parenti, the alluring manager of a rival firm whose romantic advances may dispel his melancholy mood. However, the troubled sleuth's personal dilemmas resurface when he is called to investigate the death of a retired doctor found murdered in his own home. Crime drama, starrng Michele Riondino. In Italian

programme.title: Rome's Invisible City
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by Dr Michael Scott to explore the underground treasures that made Rome the powerhouse of the ancient world. Using 3D scanning technology, he uncovers the lost subterranean world of the Colosseum, as well as the aqueducts and sewers that supplied and cleansed it, and helped run civilisation's first metropolis and its empire

programme.title: Black Music Legends of the 1980s
programme.desc: Profile of the enigmatic American musician, who rose to fame in the 1980s with hits including 1999, Kiss and Raspberry Beret. As well as achieving significant commercial success around the world, he won critical acclaim for his adventurous, genre-blurring albums, such as Sign O' the Times and Around the World in a Day - while also making headlines for his sexually explicit lyrics and stage shows, as well as his legal battle to retain control of his name and music. Featuring contributions by guitarist Dez Dickerson, Public Enemy frontman Chuck D, soul singer Beverley Knight and Paisley Park label president Alan Leeds

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Part two of two. Up-close and personal examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words from hours of archived interviews, along with commentary from those closest to him. Featuring previously unseen footage from home movies and concert performances, including his legendary 1971 'retirement concert' in Los Angeles, this latter part of the tribute to the showman follows Sinatra's growth from roadhouse performer to global singing sensation

programme.title: Patagonia: Earth's Secret Paradise
programme.desc: Life in the treacherous seas that surround the South American wilderness. The 4,000-mile coast stretches from the cold waters of Cape Horn - where rockhopper penguins overcome huge challenges to raise their young - to the far north's much warmer climate, where huge elephant seals battle for position in the heat of the desert. Orcas ram-raid the beaches, grabbing seal pups to feed their young, and fishermen are also incentivised to gather the sea's bounty. Narrated by Santiago Cabrera

programme.title: Lights Up
programme.desc: A new production of The Winter's Tale by the Royal Shakespeare Company, presented as being set between 1953 and 1969 - the years of the Coronation and the moon landing. King Leontes rips his family apart with his jealousy, but grief opens his heart. Starring Joseph Kloska and Kemi-Bo Jacobs, and directed by RSC Deputy Artistic Director Erica Whyman

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Mark Horton visits Rottingdean in East Sussex where he peeks over writer Rudyard Kipling's garden wall, an act in keeping with the activities of Victorian celebrity hunters. He also examines the history of Magnus Volk's electric railway, which ran along the Brighton seafront

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Jenny finds herself under investigation as the repercussions of the Carswell case continue to rumble on, while Clark and his team get embroiled in extortion, drug smuggling and illegal immigration. Crime drama, starring Neil Pearson

programme.title: Between the Lines
programme.desc: Clark investigates the death of a youth in custody, trying to discern whether he was murdered or committed suicide. Gritty drama, starring Neil Pearson

programme.title: Scuffles, Swagger & Shakespeare: The Hidden Story of English
programme.desc: John Gallagher examines how English became the world's most widely spoken language, examining the impact of William Shakespeare. During the playwright's lifetime, English was rarely spoken outside of Britain - but the beginnings of the British Empire would see it become a global language. The programme also examines what life was like for immigrants to Elizabethan Britain

programme.title: Sold! Inside the World's Biggest Auction House
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes at Christie's to chart a year in the life of the international institution, recording the highs and lows of life in the art business, from London to Dubai, via Hong Kong and New York

programme.title: Sold! Inside the World's Biggest Auction House
programme.desc: The second of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes at Christie's, where Global President Jussi Pylkkanen and his team continue with their big push into Asia. As the company prepares for its sale to celebrate its 250th anniversary, an umbrella stand turns out to be worth millions and a Rubens appears that has not been seen in public for 150 years

programme.title: Rome's Invisible City
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by Dr Michael Scott to explore the underground treasures that made Rome the powerhouse of the ancient world. Using 3D scanning technology, he uncovers the lost subterranean world of the Colosseum, as well as the aqueducts and sewers that supplied and cleansed it, and helped run civilisation's first metropolis and its empire

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: As the Channel Tunnel's 25th anniversary collides with Brexit, meet the teams behind the scenes and beneath the seabed who keep everything on track through the longest undersea tunnel in the world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumn landscape in a palette of exotic orange and gold colours

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the evening session on day 10 from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of two second-round matches - Mark Allen v Mark Selby, and Shaun Murphy v Yan Bingtao. Three-time champion Selby was pushed to the limit by Shaun Murphy's first-round conqueror Noppon Saengkham at this stage last year, eventually prevailing 13-12, but was eliminated in the semi-finals when he was on the wrong side of a deciding frame against Ronnie O'Sullivan

programme.title: Lights Up
programme.desc: Yasmin Joseph's play set during the 2017 Notting Hill Carnival, following two women fighting to preserve tradition and their own personal space. Filmed at the Harold Pinter Theatre

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people

programme.title: Colour: The Spectrum of Science
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski tells the story of scientific endeavour, revealing the secrets of the universe as she takes to the skies in a jumbo jet, equipped with an infra-red telescope. She explores how the birds and the bees, who can see ultraviolet, view the world, and looks at how x-rays can be used to harness colours and image the human body as never before, thereby opening up new ways to treat disease

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Megan is a wildlife expert who campaigns for animal rights and has a passion for shark conservation. Her mission is to look into the threats facing sharks in British waters and around the globe, from the deadly fin trade to industrial fishing, which have caused shark population numbers to fall dramatically in recent decades. One species she is keen to know more about is the blue shark, millions of which are caught each year for their fins, which can fetch thousands of dollars in Asia and have also ended up in Chinese restaurants in the UK. Megan also investigates the story behind a recent report that one of the nation’s favourite takeaways – fish and chips – has been linked to a species of shark which is listed as globally vulnerable and is on the brink of extinction in British waters. Megan meets up with marine biologists and shark conservationists to find out more about the dangers to British sharks, and explores ways in which sharks and other fish species can be protected. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is are some scented lavender bags, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are smelly. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate smelly objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The children’s nursery rhyme Ring A Ring O' Roses features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring smelly things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.
