programme.desc: Love Monster is excited to start a Challenge Yourself Day adventure with his friends. He’s planned a mountain hike with a special sunset surprise for them at the peak. At the base of the mountain, Love Monster hands out equipment to Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and Book Cub. But when he tries to talk through his plan and warm-up, his friends play with their equipment and have fun doing their own exercises. A little frustrated, Love Monster blows his whistle to get their attention and says it’s time to start climbing.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Pug is being competitive when he cleans the bookshop. He and Dog join a race in Australia. They help brother and sister Kangaroos who are very competitive, but get stuck in the mud. The race is won by a Koala on a skateboard who overtakes everyone.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Kyra and Khloe play at being a fairytale queen and princess, but when Khloe eats a pretend magic apple, she falls into a deep sleep. Will the Queen rescue her? Later, they enjoy a walk in the park with Mum, and there is more pretend play, with leaf monsters and crocodiles! Back at home, Kyra and Khloe become chefs and help Mum make a special dinner for the whole family.

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels 49 million years back in time to find out what colour the eggs of a giant prehistoric bird were and must protect a chick against a colony of supersized ants

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: It’s lunchtime on Bluey's back deck! But every BBQ needs a side salad, so Bingo’s relaxing sit-down will have to wait until she finds every colour of pepper her cousins want!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly promises to return a missing doll to its owner, but how can she find out where the owner lives? James helps out, and they make a surprising discovery

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Mrs Hobbs interviews for a cat sitter for George, and Waffle wants the job.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: But even though it's sunny when they leave the house, a rainy weather forecast threatens her plans. Luckily for her, Gran Gran has come prepared!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah & Duck join Scooter Boy at the cinema to watch his favourite film, but the sound doesn't seem to be working

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: When magical toy Pepi Nana wakes up in the night, she writes a letter to the moon

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny suggests Mother should have a go at creating a tune, but she gets very flustered and rushes off to her garden

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle decides to sleep in Upsy Daisy's bed while she plays with a ball in the garden

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Fantastic Beasts actor Eddie Redmayne reads The Dressing-Up Dad by Maudie Smith and Paul Howard

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt and chefs Richard Bainbridge and Georgina Hayden are joined by special guest Bill Bailey. Drinks expert Helen McGinn recommends wines via video link

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain explores the culinary traditions of the Peak District and surrounding areas, beginning by putting her own twist on the classic Bakewell tart. She then joins Ian Barrett to learn how to make Derbyshire oatcakes, before using them as a casing for her potato and dill samosas

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker looks ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including West Ham United v Chelsea in the Premier League and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Barry Hawkins v Kyren Wilson in round two, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Amber Gill, Rob Deering, Ben Bailey Smith and Katie Razzall to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - which are How to Train Your Dragon, Stranger Things, Richard Pryor and Dora Maar. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This episode's contestants are a father and daughter from Glasgow, two sisters from London, and a couple from Belfast. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade takes Faith for a night out to cheer her up, but ends up having her drink spiked. She is saved from being attacked, but begins to fear her deafness makes her vulnerable. Ethan realises he needs to come clean to Fenisha after hearing a patient's story of becoming estranged from her daughter. Jacob is enraged by a note left by Tina, and demands she be more professional

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose receives footage of Steve leaving Faith's house at dawn. While she realises she has lost an ally, she decides he still has his uses as an instrument of her revenge - and soon after, he disappears without trace. Mike receives news from Professor Rhys that Osian's tumour is now completely inoperable, and resigns himself to the fact that his son is going to die. Osian does not give up so easily however, and is determined to go through with the experimental treatment regardless

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the most controversial moves in Premier League history

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Nicola Sturgeon MSP, first minister of Scotland and leader of the SNP; Anas Sarwar MSP, leader of the Scottish Labour Party; Mark Carney, UN special envoy on climate and former governor of the Bank of England; and artist David Hockney. The newspapers are reviewed by BBC political correspondent Leila Nathoo and Sunday Times deputy political editor Caroline Wheeler

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: James Williams presents the latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, when ordinary people do extraordinary things

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley (Kick-off 12.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Burnley prevailed 2-1 when the sides met at Turf Moor in December thanks to goals by Ashley Barnes and Chris Wood and they will be keen to complete a double over Wolves given they could still be dragged into a relegation battle should they endure a poor end to the campaign. With analysis from Alex Scott and Danny Murphy

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letters X, Y and Z, concluding her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake

programme.title: Snowdonia 1890
programme.desc: The families begin to find survival is a struggle as their reconstruction of Victorian life nears the one-week mark. Tension rises with the Braddocks, and a letter from the outside world provokes tears for the Joneses. Narrated by Matthew Rhys

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: While on a walk from Porth Neigwl to Abersoch on the Llyn peninsula, Derek Brockway samples the jet-set life and local honey which he describes as 'blasus Iawn' - delicious. Also brushing up his Welsh language is comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean, as she goes back to school at Nant Gwrtheyrn. Meanwhile, Mo Jannah explores the mystery of an African longboat in a Welsh museum

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen presents a selection of small-screen clips featuring north Wales - a land of weekend warriors, Klingon speakers and eccentric railwaymen

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in the Nidderdale Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in North Yorkshire, helping prepare for the return of both animals and visitors to the hills. She joins a team keeping track of adders emerging from hibernation to find out why numbers are falling. On his Cotswold farm, Adam Henson's struggling with the unpredictable spring weather, and Tom Heap's investigates how a rise in pet ownership is fuelling dog thefts. Plus, wildlife film-maker Jack Perks celebrates the grayling

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit some of Britain's most sumptuous and unusual locations, attracting large crowds intent on finding out what their hidden treasures are worth

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie befriends a patient during a delivery, but is troubled by the news that she has subsequently been readmitted to the Lady Emily after collapsing unexpectedly at home. Sister Frances decides she needs to know more of the outside world if she is truly to understand her patients' needs, and Cyril and Fred come to the aid of a family who have been evicted from their home - although Violet is less than happy about their acts of charity

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: As AC-12 struggle to deal with the repercussions of tragic events, Hastings makes one final bid to uncover institutionalised corruption before his time runs out. Crime drama, starring Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Leeds United v Manchester United at Elland Road, as Leeds looked to avenge a heavy 6-2 defeat in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Aston Villa v West Bromwich Albion, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Brighton & Hove Albion and Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations team using their special off-road vehicle

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Leominster in Herefordshire, where she learns about the history of the local hat and glove industries. Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri are helping the teams

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: It's a big day for Valerie, but Karen has forgotten something important. A documentary crew visits the prison, and Daniel helps an inmate struggling to find a way out of the system

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards returns with the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps an artist to find a period property in Hampshire, and joins a tree-planting initiative to celebrate the 10th anniversary of the South Downs National Park

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, hoping to breathe new life into a leaking tent, a scandi style coffee table and a miniature greenhouse.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By Reform UK

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a team of roving reporters present topical stories from around the UK

programme.title: Hayley: Virgin Voter
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is about to cast her first ever vote in a Senedd election, but admits she knows very little about the day-to-day running of Welsh politics. She sets out on a road trip that will provde a crash course in how Wales is run, seeking expert advice on basic questions and discovering how the £17million budget is actually spent

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Rainie and Stuart weigh up the pros and cons of Bernie being a surrogate, Zack offers to help Nancy get a job and the Carters throw a surprise picnic for Shirley, where Linda overhears her daughter calling Tamwar. Billy meets Janet's agent Estelle, who takes an shine to him and invites him for a drink. Sue Holderness (Only Fools and Horses' Marlene) joins the cast

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: A party of German guests comes to stay and Basil resolves to ensure everyone is on their best behaviour. Regrettably, a nasty case of concussion sustained during a fire drill ensures his plans come crashing down. Starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Welsh Communist Party

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: First episode of a new sitcom, co-written by and starring Rose Matafeo. A drunken New Year's eve hook-up becomes far more complicated for Jessie when she discovers her one night stand is actually a film star. What she thought would become an amusing anecdote soon turns into something else

programme.title: Dreaming Whilst Black
programme.desc: One-off drama about a young aspiring filmmaker trapped in his recruitment job. When he bumps into an old friend from film school who is now working at a production company, he gets the chance that he has been waiting for. Unfortunately, she fixes him a once-in-a-lifetime pitching opportunity which clashes with a career-saving presentation - forcing him to choose between going all in or giving up his dream entirely. Co-written by and starring Adjani Salmon

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Swimming great Rebecca Adlington, former England cricketer Jonathan Trott, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey and Olympic hockey gold medallist Kate Richardson-Walsh join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.desc: Judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers check head to the East of Scotland to check out properties in Aberdeenshire and St Andrews, including a rural farm steading conversion, a silvery city apartment, and a home with a garden at its heart

programme.desc: A party of German guests comes to stay and Basil resolves to ensure everyone is on their best behaviour. Regrettably, a nasty case of concussion sustained during a fire drill ensures his plans come crashing down. Starring John Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs

programme.title: Greta Thunberg: A Year to Change the World
programme.desc: The Covid-19 pandemic reveals the enormity of the challenge that climate change poses, as the drop in emissions during lockdown proves far from enough to get the world back on track. She explores new measures to reduce the level of emissions further, visiting a tree plantation and learning about a machine that sucks carbon dioxide out of the air. She also explains the everyday choices that members of the public can make to reduce their environmental impact

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler explain and analyse the latest news from the General Election campaign trail

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: First episode of a new sitcom, co-written by and starring Rose Matafeo. A drunken New Year's eve hook-up becomes far more complicated for Jessie when she discovers her one night stand is actually a film star. What she thought would become an amusing anecdote soon turns into something else

programme.title: Dreaming Whilst Black
programme.desc: One-off drama about a young aspiring filmmaker trapped in his recruitment job. When he bumps into an old friend from film school who is now working at a production company, he gets the chance that he has been waiting for. Unfortunately, she fixes him a once-in-a-lifetime pitching opportunity which clashes with a career-saving presentation - forcing him to choose between going all in or giving up his dream entirely. Co-written by and starring Adjani Salmon

programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the recent series. Among those chatting to the host are George Clooney, Gal Gadot, Tom Hanks, Jessica Chastain, Hugh Grant, Dolly Parton, Samuel L Jackson, Kylie Minogue, Arsene Wenger, Nicola Adams, Dua Lipa, Bruce Springsteen, Kristin Scott Thomas, Nicole Kidman, Gary Barlow, Viola Davis, Michael J Fox, Tina Fey, Emily Blunt, Jamie Dornan, Mariah Carey and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt and chefs Richard Bainbridge and Georgina Hayden are joined by special guest Bill Bailey. Drinks expert Helen McGinn recommends wines via video link

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain explores the culinary traditions of the Peak District and surrounding areas, beginning by putting her own twist on the classic Bakewell tart. She then joins Ian Barrett to learn how to make Derbyshire oatcakes, before using them as a casing for her potato and dill samosas

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker looks ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including West Ham United v Chelsea in the Premier League and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City in the Carabao Cup final

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, including the conclusion of Barry Hawkins v Kyren Wilson in round two, and the start of Mark Allen v Mark Selby

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Amber Gill, Rob Deering, Ben Bailey Smith and Katie Razzall to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - which are How to Train Your Dragon, Stranger Things, Richard Pryor and Dora Maar. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz show, in which Bonnie Langford, Arthur Smith, Annabel Giles, Tony Hawks, Diane Louise Jordan, Melinda Messenger, Les Dennis and Richard Herring try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This episode's contestants are a father and daughter from Glasgow, two sisters from London, and a couple from Belfast. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ronan Keating, as they assist a father and son from Crawley in picking out the best singers - and get a few dance lessons in return

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade takes Faith for a night out to cheer her up, but ends up having her drink spiked. She is saved from being attacked, but begins to fear her deafness makes her vulnerable. Ethan realises he needs to come clean to Fenisha after hearing a patient's story of becoming estranged from her daughter. Jacob is enraged by a note left by Tina, and demands she be more professional

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose receives footage of Steve leaving Faith's house at dawn. While she realises she has lost an ally, she decides he still has his uses as an instrument of her revenge - and soon after, he disappears without trace. Mike receives news from Professor Rhys that Osian's tumour is now completely inoperable, and resigns himself to the fact that his son is going to die. Osian does not give up so easily however, and is determined to go through with the experimental treatment regardless

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Chelsea at London Stadium. Added spice is added to the London derby due to both teams being in the hunt for a top-four finish, and the Blues will have been looking for a repeat of their 3-0 win in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Liverpool v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Friday's contest between Arsenal and Everton

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the most controversial moves in Premier League history

programme.title: War of the Worlds (2005)
programme.desc: A divorced father's rare weekend with his two estranged children descends into horror when alien war machines rise from beneath Earth and begin to systematically destroy every human being in their path - leaving the man with the dangerous task of getting his kids to safety. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi thriller, based on HG Wells' novel, with Tom Cruise, Dakota Fanning, Justin Chatwin, Miranda Otto and Tim Robbins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by Nicola Sturgeon MSP, first minister of Scotland and leader of the SNP; Anas Sarwar MSP, leader of the Scottish Labour Party; Mark Carney, UN special envoy on climate and former governor of the Bank of England; and artist David Hockney. The newspapers are reviewed by BBC political correspondent Leila Nathoo and Sunday Times deputy political editor Caroline Wheeler

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her favourite tales of faith, hope and spirituality, when ordinary people do extraordinary things

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a flat in Forest Hill, London, and a terraced houses Nantwich, Cheshire, before speaking to their new owners

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley (Kick-off 12.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Burnley prevailed 2-1 when the sides met at Turf Moor in December thanks to goals by Ashley Barnes and Chris Wood and they will be keen to complete a double over Wolves given they could still be dragged into a relegation battle should they endure a poor end to the campaign. With analysis from Alex Scott and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in the Warwickshire countryside helping a couple with a £750,000 budget find their ideal home, where an open-plan interior and a generous garden are priorities

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is in the Nidderdale Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in North Yorkshire, helping prepare for the return of both animals and visitors to the hills. She joins a team keeping track of adders emerging from hibernation to find out why numbers are falling. On his Cotswold farm, Adam Henson's struggling with the unpredictable spring weather, and Tom Heap's investigates how a rise in pet ownership is fuelling dog thefts. Plus, wildlife film-maker Jack Perks celebrates the grayling

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit some of Britain's most sumptuous and unusual locations, attracting large crowds intent on finding out what their hidden treasures are worth

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie befriends a patient during a delivery, but is troubled by the news that she has subsequently been readmitted to the Lady Emily after collapsing unexpectedly at home. Sister Frances decides she needs to know more of the outside world if she is truly to understand her patients' needs, and Cyril and Fred come to the aid of a family who have been evicted from their home - although Violet is less than happy about their acts of charity

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: As AC-12 struggle to deal with the repercussions of tragic events, Hastings makes one final bid to uncover institutionalised corruption before his time runs out. Crime drama, starring Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Leeds United v Manchester United at Elland Road, as Leeds looked to avenge a heavy 6-2 defeat in December's reverse fixture. Plus, Aston Villa v West Bromwich Albion, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Burnley and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Brighton & Hove Albion and Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Joe Sugg and Richard Madeley join regular panellists Bill Bailey, Emily Atack and Judi Love in trying to guess which of the four Clares is the real owner of a property in Guildford, Surrey. Those trying to pass themselves off as the lady of the manor include a former air hostess and a 'Klara' from Slovakia, who says most people think she is the cleaner not the homeowner. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood joins everyday hard grafters who keep the country's essential infrastructure running, beginning in Belfast harbour, where ferries leave for the UK mainland. His mentor is operations manager Howard Hillis, who has worked at the harbour for the last 21 years. Dom is thrown in at the deep end, spending 24 hours at sea, learning the ropes alongside the captain and many of his crew, before transferring to the historic Harland & Wolff dockyards, where the Titanic was built, to assist with the essential maintenance of a ship in dry dock

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A fisherman has had a stroke in a remote location and needs to be rescued by the Special Operations team using their special off-road vehicle

programme.desc: We explore how biases built into everyday technologies impact people's lives and how the tech industry is trying to address it. programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: Talking Movies reports on the excitement at Union Station in Los Angeles, which is co-hosting this year's Academy Awards. Several big name stars tell the programme team how they view the 2021 Oscars contest, which has the most diverse field of contenders ever. British actor Riz Ahmed discusses the significance of becoming the first Muslim to be nominated for a best actor Oscar. Also, celebrated Danish film-maker Thomas Vinterberg discusses his drinking drama Another Round, favoured to win for best international feature film. The programme will also reveal what millions of viewers around the world can expect to see when they tune in to watch this in-person Oscars ceremony, which has been significantly reinvented because of the global pandemic.

programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: Talking Movies reports on the excitement at Union Station in Los Angeles, which is co-hosting this year's Academy Awards. Several big name stars tell the programme team how they view the 2021 Oscars contest, which has the most diverse field of contenders ever. British actor Riz Ahmed discusses the significance of becoming the first Muslim to be nominated for a best actor Oscar. Also, celebrated Danish film-maker Thomas Vinterberg discusses his drinking drama Another Round, favoured to win for best international feature film. The programme will also reveal what millions of viewers around the world can expect to see when they tune in to watch this in-person Oscars ceremony, which has been significantly reinvented because of the global pandemic.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Under cover of Covid, the Amazon rainforest is under attack. Deforestation is at levels not seen for more than a decade. Brazil's president, Jair Bolsonaro, talks of opening the forest up to development while the environmental police are under attack from loggers. Our World has obtained a recording of the environment minister talking about using the cover of Covid to 'change all the rules' in the Amazon. Justin Rowlatt goes on a mission to find out how a tribe he visited a decade ago is faring in the face of this assault.

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Everybody is busy preparing for the Grand Concert, which takes place each year. Grandad Island is visiting and everyone is gearing up for a great evening, but things don't go quite as planned.

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The starfish tells us how they can stand, walk and eat - even though they don't have any bones to hold them up

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: When the forest friends all hear that the wishing fruit is about to ripen, they all race to be the first to pick one - an achievement that will see them gain a very special power

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Famous pop star Brad Lee is due in town, but presently stuck at sea as his tour ship has broken down. Pàdraig is sent to rescue him in the SDS boat, but Brad isn't quite as comfortable on such a small boat

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: A flood leaves the Moomins without a house

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: There are many tasteful things to behold - except for Coinneach Clogaid

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: The summer has come to an end and Matilda must go back to the capital, moving away from her friends, especially Charlie

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Ria from Lewis tells us about her favourite hobbies which include art, swimming and baking

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Am BFG (The BFG) (1989)
programme.desc: A friendly giant befriends a lonely little girl and transports her to a strange land. Unfortunately, a number of the other giants there have acquired a taste for eating children. Determined to stop their nefarious habit, the pair set out to charm the Queen of England into putting her armed forces at their disposal. Animated adventure, based on Roald Dahl's classic tale, with the voices of Donald Smith, Catriona Lexy Campbell and Donna Morrison

programme.title: Siubhlachan (Time Traveller)
programme.desc: A girl inherits a watch from her grandfather that allows her to time-travel, but discovers dark forces are intent on taking the device for themselves

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Hillwalker Colin MacLeod visits Skye and Applecross as he continues his investigation into the history of place names in the Highlands

programme.title: Gaisgich Oga an Darna Cogaidh
programme.desc: Eva is already an orphan when she arrives in the Auschwitz concentration camp. She hopes to find friends there from the Theresienstadt children's choir

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell hosts the Irish-language country music show from the Town Hall in Newry, with Jim Devine and his special guest Maighréad Ní Dhomhnaill taking to the stage

programme.title: Bana-Churaidhean/Warrior Women
programme.desc: Docu-drama series on history's most iconic women fighters. This episode follows the life of a fictional Roman gladiatrix called Ardala

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum travels across Loch Awe to Kilchurn Castle, looking for the perfect light to take a special photo

programme.title: World Pipe Band Championships
programme.desc: Catrìona MacNeil introduces some of the best bands competing at Grade 1 in the World Pipe Band Championships from previous years

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: The Seumas Egan Project play a tune and also perform the song Days of War

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon reads a parable and introduces fitting hymns and psalms - some recorded especially for Alleluia. Rev David Nicolson and Kathleen Reddy each present their chosen reading from the Bible and related reflections

programme.title: Leugh Mi (Book Show)
programme.desc: Anna Macleod is in conversation with two guests each week about their favourite books. This week, she is joined by Rev Donald Michael MacInnes and Joe Sanders

programme.title: Wild Atlantic Way
programme.desc: Anne Lundon takes to the road for a journey along Ireland's spectacular Wild Atlantic Way

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: The DIY expert takes on more projects, starting by building a treehouse for two boys

programme.desc: Calum travels across Loch Awe to Kilchurn Castle, looking for the perfect light to take a special photo

programme.title: Opry an Iúir

programme.title: Eadar na Dha: 1918-1935/Clash of Futures: 1918-1935
programme.desc: A look back at Ireland in the aftermath of the First World War, as old conflicts arise over the country's future and other European powers gather for the Paris Peace Conference

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean heads to Loch Morlich, a freshwater loch near Aviemore for a frosty swim

programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Leugh le Linda
programme.desc: Linda Macleod travels to Benbecula where she reads Oidhche Mhath, Sam with nursery school pupils

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: It is springtime and Rita and Crocodile are trying out their new boots in the muddy puddles

programme.title: Baile Mhuilinn
programme.desc: Gaelic songs and stories for children learning to read, featuring the letter E

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Sea Pen tells us while they look like plants, they are definitely animals - little creatures who join together to form one organism

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: The duo goes skating and learns about ice - including the fact that it can melt

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack reads Famh agus Èirigh-na-grèine by Jeanne Willis, illustrated Sarah Fox-Davies and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: In his new play, Papà Mùman tells of his adventures as a young Mùman

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Mortimer from the future takes Matilda to the Paper Port of the future. Tropecio Ferguson is the absolute master there because he has the Magical Coconut powers now

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Kerry Anne Macleod and Allan MacDonald bring science to life with fun experiments able to be attempted at home. Among their latest challenges are making water travel down a tightrope and how to grow a salad

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Stornoway-based vet Joanna Jackson is busy with lambing in Ness and Barvas, while in Aberdeenshire Scott Rigg is in surgery with a Bernese mountain dog and a collie

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Poet Peter MacKay talks about poetry and its importance. He discusses how Lewis poet Iain Crichton Smith has influenced him and reads Tha thu air Aigeann M'Inntinn

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar visits Edinburgh, where she draws a portrait of Iagan MacNeil, who has forged a successful career in the media - as journalist, newsreader and in public relations

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Following young dancers from around Scotland as they take part in the World Highland Dancing Championships

programme.title: Na Trads
programme.desc: Another chance to hear music by The Scott Wood Band, Session A9, Tryst, Chris Stout & Catriona MacKay and Kinnaris Quintet, amongst others

programme.title: Am Posadh Afraganach/The African Wedding
programme.desc: Humanitarian Gemma Steele and her husband Sean MacKinnon make a special trip for a second wedding celebration with her other family in Kenya

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Ruaridh makes a tasty beer-battered fish, while Uisdean prepares twice-cooked chips to go with it, and then makes Scampi and “Clampi” in tempura batter

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is housesitting for Weasel. When he sees him returning from the grocery store with Fizz, he thinks they're burglars!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are at the supermarket buying ingredients to make the world's best cake. They bump into Magoo, who thinks he's at a carnival!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is lost in a pyramid but he is in fact in a big conference room where Fizz is about to perform a stand-up act.

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Inspired by a documentary on Buckingham Palace, Bean sets out to decorate his room in royal style, and landlady Mrs Wicket suffers delusions of grandeur

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean gets involved in a marrow-growing contest with his neighbour and, as the competition hots up, he tries all sorts of weird and wonderful methods to win. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Grandpa Max's alma mater asks him to use the Omni-Copter to transport an important exhibit to an exotic nature reserve but when the exhibit turns out to be a rare and very large baby moth Ben must keep Tim Buktu from stealing the moth while learning that sometimes you need to use finesse and not force to solve hard problems.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: More and more people are disappearing in Ninjago City, while the ninja scramble to unlock the secrets of the Prime Empire arcade game. They decide to take the game to Cyrus Borg, hoping he can help them decode the programming and release all the players who have been taken

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: The ninja's materialize inside Prime Empire and go immediately on the run when they discover a squadron of Red Visors waiting for them!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe and actor and singer Tom Read Wilson join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by singer and West End star Kimberley Walsh and there are recipes for seabass, sole and a rack of lamb. Guest chefs Elizabeth Haigh and Paul Ainsworth also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef is joined by actor and entertainer John Barrowman and prepares a selection of sweets and treats, starting with his cheesy, gooey souffle Welsh rarebit muffins. Former Great British Bake Off semi-finalist Briony May Williams makes a miso caramel and pear chocolate layer cake, while Olly Smith picks a range of drinks that are perfect for an indulgent moment,

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the region of Jura, where a local farmer shows him around his orchards and teaches him about the region's apples and pears, which James uses as the accompaniment in a barbecued duck. He also visits Fort des Rousses, the old military stronghold that is now used for the making of Comte, one of the country's most popular cheeses

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of jumps finale day at Sandown Park, including the 3.05 Celebration Chase and 3.40 bet365 Gold Cup. Plus, flat racing from Leicester and Haydock Park. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The Ruddock brothers come up against Olympian Colin Jackson and his clan. They'll face a variety of general knowledge-based rounds under the watchful eye of Anne Hegerty, as they attempt to make it through to the semi finals. Who will succeed and get closer to the chance of winning £25,000 for their chosen charity?

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Dave, Linda, Andrea and Jonathan pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Southampton for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Game of Talents

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Martin Kemp's brother Gary joins him and Roman for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh creates an accessible garden in Derbyshire for Jack Parrott, a D-Day veteran approaching his 100th birthday. The team seeks out advice on clever new ways to create raised beds and patio-size greenhouses to find ways of allowing Jack to once again indulge in his life-long love of gardening

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan is joined by chef Ainsley Harriott brings the Mediterranean spirit to Manor Farm, while horticulturist David Domoney has smart new ways to tidy up gardens. Plus, the search and rescue dogs that will be keeping people safe this summer

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef is joined by actor and entertainer John Barrowman and prepares a selection of sweets and treats, starting with his cheesy, gooey souffle Welsh rarebit muffins. Former Great British Bake Off semi-finalist Briony May Williams makes a miso caramel and pear chocolate layer cake, while Olly Smith picks a range of drinks that are perfect for an indulgent moment,

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up Stephen Mulhern and Emma Willis to try to uncover the secret talents of mystery performers based only on their appearance and some intriguing clues, with big money up for grabs

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Southampton for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Babe (1995)
programme.desc: A kindly farmer wins a cute piglet, which some greedy humans and jealous animals hope will end up as the Christmas roast. Undaunted, the young pig proves himself useful on the farm and enlists the aid of the resident dog in learning how to herd sheep. Family comedy, starring James Cromwell and Magda Szubanski, with Christine Cavanaugh, Miriam Margolyes and Hugo Weaving among those providing the voices of the animal characters

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The body of farmer Alex Preston is found in the middle of the sacred Crowcall stone circle in Midsomer Mow, with new medical examiner Dr Kate Wilding confirming that the victim was disembowelled but that a head injury was the likely cause of death. Suspicion initially falls on local druids led by high priests Ezra Canning and Leticia Clifford, since Preston's plans to plough Gorse Meadow would have barred their access to the site. Barnaby and Jones are joined by Wilding to investigate, and as the case wears on, the inspector becomes convinced greed rather than spiritualism is the motive. Starring Neil Dudgeon, Jason Hughes and Tamzin Malleson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Sam Quek, Basil Brush, Andi Peters and Charlie Higson pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as Robert Rinder, Josie Gibson and Greg Rutherford answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Quantum of Solace (2008)
programme.desc: James Bond sets out on a personal mission of vengeance as he pursues the secret criminal organisation Quantum. His search leads him to the head of an ecological corporation, who is plotting to orchestrate a coup in a Latin American country to get his hands on a precious natural resource. Spy thriller sequel, starring Daniel Craig, Olga Kurylenko, Judi Dench, Gemma Arterton and Mathieu Amalric

programme.title: After the Storm: America's Enemy Within?
programme.desc: Robert Moore travels across the United States to track down the perpetrators he encountered while filming during the storming of the US Capitol by a mob in January. He hears about their motivations and grievances and asks if they regret their roles, and examines what the fallout for America was from this deadly invasion

programme.title: And Finally - With Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The presenter's favourite trademark news stories from across his 30-year career at ITN, which offered a glimpse into the nation's lighter, quirkier moments. He catches up with some of the protagonists, including the man who turned his lounge into the Starship Enterprise, and the girl who was christened with 217 names

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sharon sets her plan into motion, Dev arranges a summit meeting with Corey's father, and Chesney acts as peacemaker for Fiz and Tyrone

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan is joined by Twiggy, who pays tribute to the Great British seaside, while horticulturist David Domoney selects garden furniture to help people entertain this summer. Plus, Alan goes behind the scenes at one of the UK's leading farm parks

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sharon steals from Gail as pressure mounts. Dev drives Asha further towards Corey. George disapproves of Todd's business methods

programme.title: Viewpoint
programme.desc: Manchester primary school teacher Gemma Hillman goes missing and DCs Martin Young and Stella Beckett are given the task of putting her boyfriend - the prime suspect - under surveillance. Clarke sets up his observation post in the home of single mum and secret voyeur Zoe Sterling, whose windows offer a panoramic view of Westbury Square and the home Gemma shared with Greg Sullivan. Crime drama, starring Noel Clarke, Alexandra Roach and Bronagh Waugh

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Accused of Murdering Our Son - The Steven Clark Story
programme.desc: Steven Clark, 23, disappeared in mysterious circumstances following a family walk with his mother on the 28th December, 1992. In September 2020, his parents Doris, 81, and Charles, 78, were arrested on suspicion of murder following a review by police from the Cleveland and North Yorkshire Cold Case Unit. Presenter and ex-detective Mark Williams-Thomas takes a look at the case

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Three pairs of contestants call upon their powers of deduction in a battle of words. programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Inspired by a documentary on Buckingham Palace, Bean sets out to decorate his room in royal style, and landlady Mrs Wicket suffers delusions of grandeur

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean gets involved in a marrow-growing contest with his neighbour and, as the competition hots up, he tries all sorts of weird and wonderful methods to win. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Grandpa Max's alma mater asks him to use the Omni-Copter to transport an important exhibit to an exotic nature reserve but when the exhibit turns out to be a rare and very large baby moth Ben must keep Tim Buktu from stealing the moth while learning that sometimes you need to use finesse and not force to solve hard problems.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: More and more people are disappearing in Ninjago City, while the ninja scramble to unlock the secrets of the Prime Empire arcade game. They decide to take the game to Cyrus Borg, hoping he can help them decode the programming and release all the players who have been taken

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: The ninja's materialize inside Prime Empire and go immediately on the run when they discover a squadron of Red Visors waiting for them!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Comedian Sara Pascoe and actor and singer Tom Read Wilson join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by singer and West End star Kimberley Walsh and there are recipes for seabass, sole and a rack of lamb. Guest chefs Elizabeth Haigh and Paul Ainsworth also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: The chef is joined by actor and entertainer John Barrowman and prepares a selection of sweets and treats, starting with his cheesy, gooey souffle Welsh rarebit muffins. programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the region of Jura, where a local farmer shows him around his orchards and teaches him about the region's apples and pears, which James uses as the accompaniment in a barbecued duck. He also visits Fort des Rousses, the old military stronghold that is now used for the making of Comte, one of the country's most popular cheeses

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of jumps finale day at Sandown Park, including the 3.05 Celebration Chase and 3.40 bet365 Gold Cup. Plus, flat racing from Leicester and Haydock Park. With analysis from AP McCoy and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The Ruddock brothers come up against Olympian Colin Jackson and his clan. Who will succeed and get closer to the chance of winning £25,000 for their chosen charity?

programme.title: Mutiny on the Buses (1972)
programme.desc: Chirpy bus driver Stan Butler lands himself in trouble as he struggles to raise enough money to marry his girlfriend, and on top of training his unemployed brother-in-law for a job at the depot he has to contend with an officious new manager. Comedy based on the sitcom, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis, Bob Grant, Michael Robbins, Anna Karen and Doris Hare

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When a man's charred remains are found after an explosion, Foyle's investigations lead him to a top secret intelligence organisation. With murder and deception rife among its members, the race is on to find out the truth, but the detective's discoveries threaten his future career. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen with guests Angela Thorne, Ronald Pickup and Samuel West

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A mischievous trickster finally gets his comeuppance when his body is found floating below the weir in Midsomer Deverell. The extent of his double life starts to emerge when three family friends and a long-lost niece produce wills claiming his fortune, and his collection of erotic photographs from the 1960s gives Barnaby and Jones the key to the tangled relationships and feuds blighting the family

programme.title: The Suspicions of Mr Whicher
programme.desc: Whicher is hired by his one-time political master, former home secretary Edward Shore, to investigate threats made against his son Charles, who has recently returned to London from India with his young family. Shore fears entanglement in a scandal could have devastating repercussions for his career and insists the detective carry out the investigation without involving his former Met colleagues, even after a murder occurs. Whicher's pursuit of justice takes him to the most dangerous corners of the Docklands, but also brings him into contact with a woman who starts to melt his long-frozen heart. Victorian crime drama, starring Paddy Considine, with Nicholas Jones, John Heffernan and Tim Pigott-Smith

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A pregnant junior doctor is found beaten to death in a hospital car park, and her boyfriend Joe Nash becomes the prime suspect when he cannot provide an alibi. Brooks and Devlin begin to think Nash is having an affair with his therapist, but what initially seems like a crime of passion unravels into a conspiracy involving senior government officials, with Steel and Phillips risking their careers to find the truth. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Matthew McNulty star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid informs Jean he has been given a temporary promotion at work, but she soon begins to doubt what he says. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: On the Buses

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is having difficulty supporting the whole family on his meagre earnings. Comedy, starring Reg Varney

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rishi is excited about his birthday, Tracy is disappointed, leaving Nate guilty, and Vinny is guilt wracked

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle responds to complaints of sabotage at an RAF burns hospital and uncovers adultery and murder within the medical unit. As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Two grieving widows lay rival claims to a corpse found washed up on the beach, each insisting it is the body of her missing husband. While it is obvious only one can be telling the truth, the detective finds it tricky to discern which of the women is lying - and why. Drama, guest starring Eleanor Bron, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of a sheep farmer leads Wycliffe into the heart of a rural community whose apparently tranquil surface conceals a desperate struggle for survival. It is not long before he uncovers disturbing evidence suggesting foul play. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A mysterious fire that killed an artist with links to political activists leaves the police baffled, while Bright finds himself short-staffed when a stomach bug puts most of his men on sick leave. The situation becomes more serious when a housewife dies of the same condition. Endeavour investigates a family-run supermarket, believing the infection may have stemmed from their stock being deliberately contaminated, and discovers dark secrets that may have a connection to the unexplained blaze

programme.title: The Queen's Green Planet
programme.desc: Documentary following Her Majesty the Queen and an ambitious legacy project to create a global network of protected forests, spanning the 53 countries of the Commonwealth. The film also features a conversation between the Queen and Sir David Attenborough in the gardens of Buckingham Palace last summer, with the subjects ranging from climate change to conkers and birthday gifts. There is also footage of the members of the royal family involved so far in making the Queen's Commonwealth Canopy a reality, including Prince Harry planting trees in the Caribbean and Prince William in Canada's Great Bear Rainforest

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama starring Brenda Blethyn. The detective investigates a fire that rips through a holiday park on the Northumberland coast, destroying three cabins. A young woman's body is discovered and the resort's owner, Jim Viner, fears the victim is his sister Deena when he returns from a weekend away with his daughter Clare. With Jim's suspicions confirmed, a police report then reveals Clare's boyfriend Ryan and his friends had been partying near the scene hours before the blaze started, but where are they now, and where is Deena's best friend Sally? Co-starring Kenny Doughty, Cush Jumbo and Jon Morrison

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present two stories of women trying to bring their families back together. Mother and daughter Val and Marisa Moorhouse from West Yorkshire are united in their search for their missing son and brother Stephen, whom Val had adopted in 1977, when she felt she could not afford to give him the life he deserved. Meanwhile, 37-year-old Sam from Surrey is searching not only for her birth mother, but also her sister

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Detective Superintendent Elizabeth Bancroft is running an operation to against a vicious gang and adopts dubious methods to bring down the brothers who run it. Meanwhile, DS Katherine Stevens is assigned to a cold murder case and finds that there's more to it than it seems - and that Bancroft has some secrets in her past that may prove difficult to hide. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, with Faye Marsay, Art Malik and Amara Khan

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips
