programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Bonnie and Danny take a cookery class run by a noted chef, but when Danny wins the chance to be a guest sous chef, Bonnie is determined to prove that she's the better cook. Tucker advises Riley to make up a fake boyfriend in order to fix her friendship with Danny

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben and Riley play a game of 'shot and score', where they compete to see who can kiss a stranger first. Riley sets her sights on a handsome bar patron, while Ben hits it off with another customer. However, when Riley is horrified when her one-night stand turns out to be her new boss. Bonnie uses Riley's office for her real estate business, and Danny makes a big investment in his future

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Advertising executive Andre 'Dre''Johnson is happily anticipating a promotion that would make him his company's first black senior vice-president, while his wife Rainbow, an anaesthetist, is looking forward to their successful future. Pilot of the comedy following the lives of an upper-middle-class African-American family, starring Anthony Anderson, Tracee Ellis Ross and Laurence Fishburne.

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre is upset to find out that Rainbow has already told Andre Jr about adulthood because she thinks he's too much of a prude to do it. However, he soon regrets giving his son his own version of 'the talk' when Andre Jr starts to drive him mad with non-stop questions about sex. Comedy, starring Anthony Anderson

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel begins a relationship with a younger man, while Lennox faces a challenge from one of her classmates when she takes up the post of editor of the school blog. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel's new boyfriend George throws a party so that she can meet his friends, while Joe confronts his ex-wife after she takes her date to a location that was significant in their relationship. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys have fun at the expense of Penny's boyfriend Zack, but later try to make amends by enlisting his help with the New Year's costume competition at the comic-book store. Comedy, starring Kaley Cuoco and Simon Helberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard's ingenious idea for a new smartphone app puts pressure on his friendship with Sheldon, who vies with him for control of the project. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry enlists his friends to help him break up Beverly's social group in order to get more of his mother's attention, but when big arguments ensue he realises he's made a major error. Adam's attempts to trick Murray into loving the theatre backfire

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Hoping to enjoy a true spring break experience, Erica and Barry pay a visit to Pops in Florida, but the jaunt isn't what they expected. Adam tells the truth to his parents when he has a mishap with Beverly's new coat, but he gets a surprising reaction

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Holt and Peralta contract mumps during an investigation, so the pair elect to be quarantined together in the hope that they can use the time to solve the case. Diaz tries to help Boyle through the mourning process for his dog, and Jeffords is forced to clean up Hitchcock and Scully's mess. Oscar Nunez guest stars

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago are excited to go on their first real holiday together, but their perfectly planned cruise ship itinerary is thwarted when they discover that an old adversary is on board. Boyle and Diaz vie for the same apartment, while Gina and Jeffords help Captain Holt to survive a visit from his sister Debbie

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard returns from his research expedition in the North Sea and undergoes an adjustment period with Sheldon. Howard experiences an unusual emotional reaction as a result of his relationship with his mother. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends compete in a scavenger hunt designed by Raj to test their scientific knowledge and life skills, but the game brings out the best and worst in everyone. As the hunt progresses, Howard and Amy begin to bond over their shared musical tastes, while Penny and Sheldon work together as a team and Bernadette becomes fiercely competitive

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: In order to achieve a better work-life balance, Mel agrees to put Joe in charge of renovating her home - but his management style leaves a lot to be desired. Meanwhile, Lennox's school drama club decides to stage a play she has written. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel is charmed by the man who designs her new kitchen cabinets, but Joe fails to see eye-to-eye with him - leading to a big argument. Meanwhile, Lennox reluctantly attends one of her school's student committees, before an encounter with the organisation's presidents bolsters her enthusiasm. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Despite Beverly's wishes, Jackie's parents allow Adam to watch horror movie A Nightmare on Elm Street, and a family disagreement ensues. Meanwhile, Erica starts hanging out at her old high school and soon realises she's not as popular as she once was

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray is angered when he finds out that Adam has no interest in auditioning for a school stage production. Meanwhile, Beverly interferes with Erica's latest career plan

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: When another man is dismissive of Dre in front of Rainbow, he worries that his manliness is in question. Pops steps in to help his son save face, impressing Ruby with his show of confidence and re-igniting their love affair

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: On the way to the family's annual skiing trip, Dre realises that his children may not appreciate the significance of Dr Martin Luther King Jr so he decides to inundate them with historical facts about the civil rights campaigner

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: After identifying the mob informant, Holt, Jake and Rosa make plans to infiltrate FBI headquarters to get the man's files, with the captain bringing in his former partner to help out. Guest starring Dennis Haysbert (24's President Palmer)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Officers at the precinct drop everything to intervene when they suspect that Captain Holt is in danger after their efforts to track down Adrian Pimento's hitman take an unexpected turn. Dennis Haysbert guest stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny makes an important decision after Leonard tries to help her in the aftermath of a failed role on a TV show. Meanwhile, Raj and Stuart practise talking to strangers at a shopping centre, and Sheldon studies to become a comic genius. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to relax after he is forced to take a holiday, and when Penny quits her job the duo end up spending time together. Meanwhile, Leonard struggles to be supportive of his girlfriend's career decision, Bernadette seeks Stuart's help in replacing one of Howard's comic books, and Howard and Raj provide Amy with some advice. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Gogglesprogs
programme.desc: The young TV viewers offer their honest, inimitable and ultimately hilarious take on First Dates, Nature's Weirdest Events and classic movie Dirty Dancing

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Sheldon and Howard face problems when they file for a patent for their infinite persistence gyroscope. Plus, Penny, Amy and Bernadette give Raj dating advice when Emily reaches out to him after their breakup. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: The gang leaves the Cotswolds for a country retreat in Suffolk, while Emily plans a saucy surprise for Harvey to celebrate their one-year anniversary. Miles is caught in the crossfire between Julius and Inga as accusations of flirting continue to fly

programme.title: Temptation Island
programme.desc: Temperatures on the island heat up as the connections multiply in ways none of the couples dreamed

programme.title: Pete & Sam's Reality News
programme.desc: Pete Wicks and Sam Thompson examine the latest gossip from the world of reality TV, including breaking news and segments from up-and-coming comedic and presenting talent

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is furious when the university forces him to work with Kripke, while Howard's hobby lands him in financial trouble. However, that doesn't stop him and Raj getting expensive action figures of themselves made - much to Bernadette's disapproval

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: An argument between Leonard and Sheldon over the ending of a Harry Potter book affects Amy and Penny's living arrangements. Meanwhile, Howard goes on a trip to Las Vegas with Bernadette and asks Raj to keep an eye on his mother while he is away

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on TV from the first week of September 2017, featuring The Great British Bake Off, Doctor Foster, The X Factor, The Crystal Maze, Mountain: Life at the Extreme, Cheap Cheap Cheap and the news

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Russell Howard invents sticky tape architecture, Tim Vine attempts a risky impersonation and Alex Horne receives a gift from Liza Tarbuck, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: The gang leaves the Cotswolds for a country retreat in Suffolk, while Emily plans a saucy surprise for Harvey to celebrate their one-year anniversary. Miles is caught in the crossfire between Julius and Inga as accusations of flirting continue to fly

programme.title: Temptation Island
programme.desc: Temperatures on the island heat up as the connections multiply in ways none of the couples dreamed

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys have fun at the expense of Penny's boyfriend Zack, but later try to make amends by enlisting his help with the New Year's costume competition at the comic-book store. Comedy, starring Kaley Cuoco and Simon Helberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard's ingenious idea for a new smartphone app puts pressure on his friendship with Sheldon, who vies with him for control of the project. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi discover their eldest daughter Kate has been less than truthful about her whereabouts, and they come up with a plan to catch her out

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi consider the prospect of expanding their brood with another baby, after Don and Marcy pay a visit with their newborn grandson

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits southern Italy to find out where the bergamot flavour in Earl Grey tea comes from, before meeting a man who invented a method of making tea using an aerosol. Matt Tebbutt finds out why pork scratchings aren't made from British pigs and Kate Quilton is in a factory in California to uncover the secrets of jelly beans

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Fay Lewis has dreamed of moving to Spain for 20 years. Now, with her house in the UK sold and her bags packed, she's ready to take the plunge. Jasmine Harman shows Fay five properties around the Axarquia region of the Costa del Sol for her budget of 95,000 euros

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tina Watkins has long struggled to get on the housing ladder in the UK. Now, she has decided to make the most of the incredibly good value property in Spain's Granada province and buy her first-ever home. With her best friend Jamie along to help, Jasmine Harman shows Tina just how far her £19,000 budget will stretch

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jon and Lynne Scopes from Stockport have thought about buying a property abroad for years and have viewed more than 40 apartments without success, but have at least decided their ideal area is Mijas on Spain's Costa del Sol. Jasmine Harman has the daunting task of finding them the perfect property for their £100,000 budget, but will anything meet their exacting standards?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are house hunting in Essex and East London. Kirstie is searching for grandmother Janet, who is looking for a future-proof home where she will be able to live comfortably in the years to come. Meanwhile, Phil meets first-time buyers Adam and Becky, who are struggling to find a place to start a family

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search across two countries for two homes. Kirstie is with serial renters Mike and Poppy. Mike has lived in 18 homes, but Poppy wants to put down roots and they've chosen Cheshire as the right spot for their family. However, living in a high spec rental has given them great expectations for the home they'd love to own. Phil is in north Wales, helping nature-loving Pip, who's in search of a rural retreat, a chocolate-box character cottage with a garden big enough for chickens

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former property developer Ian hosts the first dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping his super-healthy menu will win him the £1,000 prize - although it is possible his guests may be more impressed with his 12-bedroom mansion in the country. Some naked antics, a pinata and stories of millions pounds won and lost make it a night to remember

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Then-Miss Cheltenham Sophie hosts the second dinner party in Gloucestershire, hoping her evening of elegant food and pageantry will win her the £1,000 prize. The 24-year-old reveals what she does for a living to her guests, and one attendee makes a surprising confession of her own

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby-loving Carmel hosts the third dinner party in Gloucestershire, and she hopes to impress with a 'Six Nations' starter, a pancetta, king prawn and chorizo main, and a Key lime pie for dessert. An attempt to charm her guests backfires when she likens Ian to a psychotic killer, and the atmosphere takes a further knock after Chris gets on the wrong side of Sophie

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: School teacher Polly, 40, hosts the fourth night of the competition in Gloucestershire, and hopes to impress her guests with a fine-dining experience. She begins with a seafood starter before serving up a meaty main course, and isn't unnerved by the fact she hasn't had any practise cooking either dish. However, her guests show off round the table and threaten to take the attention away from the night, but Ian is highly impressed with Polly's choice of entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of exuberant hair salon owner Chris to host the final dinner party in Gloucestershire with home-made treats on the menu, including a tiger prawn starter, venison for the main and a dessert of chocolate and walnut ice-cream. Chat around the table reveals some differing opinions after the guests make a discovery in the host's living room, and the group reflect on their time together before the winner of the cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie welcome their guests to the Watersedge Guest House in Barton-on-Sea, Hampshire, but an afternoon of deer-watching leaves some of them bored. Desperate to impress their fellow B&B owners, the pair host a Hawaiian barbecue, before pulling out all the stops at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival B&B owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next day's breakfast fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: The ancient meadows get some new inhabitants when the family take delivery of some new beehives, while Matt prepares for lambing season and selects a ram with help from mum Janice. Billy the vet returns to check on the puppies before they leave for their forever homes, and the family reflects on the positive changes they have made since Janice's accident

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 84-year-old is rushed into hospital after losing control of his car and crashing into a road sign, and is in need of an emergency transfusion after his blood pressure drops dangerously low. Elsewhere, seven-year-old Reggie needs surgery to fix his broken arm, and 66-year-old Christine comes into A&E with worrying symptoms after the entire left side of her body collapses

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 61-year-old woman is airlifted to hospital after a car mounted the pavement and ran her over and doctors quickly get her into the CT scanner to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. Elsewhere, 91-year-old Sam arrives after suffering a suspected stroke, and four-month-old boy Teddy is rushed in after falling face first out of his car seat, hitting the pavement

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the popular words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and David Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Craggy Island's most famous religious relic is due to be upgraded and Ted sets about trying to ensure the ceremony runs smoothly. However, Dougal and Jack put his plans in jeopardy when they meet the three priests in charge of proceedings. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal allows himself to be led astray by rebellious new arrival Father Damien, and Ted is suspected of being the criminal mastermind behind a scandalous theft. Comedy, with Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Joe Rooney

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 84-year-old is rushed into hospital after losing control of his car and crashing into a road sign, and is in need of an emergency transfusion after his blood pressure drops dangerously low. Elsewhere, seven-year-old Reggie needs surgery to fix his broken arm, and 66-year-old Christine comes into A&E with worrying symptoms after the entire left side of her body collapses

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 61-year-old woman is airlifted to hospital after a car mounted the pavement and ran her over and doctors quickly get her into the CT scanner to ascertain the full extent of her injuries. Elsewhere, 91-year-old Sam arrives after suffering a suspected stroke, and four-month-old boy Teddy is rushed in after falling face first out of his car seat, hitting the pavement

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Craggy Island's most famous religious relic is due to be upgraded and Ted sets about trying to ensure the ceremony runs smoothly. However, Dougal and Jack put his plans in jeopardy when they meet the three priests in charge of proceedings. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a father and daughter who are turning an American school bus into a festival campervan and in Germany he visits a young father who has made a home from a grain silo. Also, after months of construction work, the moment of truth finally arrives as George and Will's floating beach hut is launched onto the open sea - and just in case it doesn't go to plan, the ever-resourceful Will has added an escape pod made from an old bath

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: George meets Matt and Kevin, whose 1930s home stands out for all the wrong reasons. Sitting alongside the elegant Victorian and Edwardian properties in south-west London, this three-up, three-down is ugly from the outside and even worse on the inside, with dark, dated and badly designed spaces. Architects Katerina Dionysopoulou and Billy Mavropoulos are brought in to make most radical transformation on the programme to date - but will their wildly ambitious poured-concrete extension prove too innovative for the owners?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits a Victorian villa in Leeds, West Yorkshire, where Jawad and Charlie are planning a master en-suite which, if the rest of their quirky renovations are anything to go by, looks set to be a mammoth task. He also helps Jason and Hannah in Brixton, south London, who are tackling an ambitious two-storey extension. The big question is - should they lose their redundant chimney breast and increase the size of the room?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition heads to Milton Keynes, where first to host is 52-year old hypnotherapist Howard, who is hoping his fancy menu will go down with his guests. However, with dishes including 'red treacle tart' his visitors are unsure what to expect, and not everyone shares the thrill when Howard introduces his 'Pendulum of Truth' to the proceedings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Louise hosts the second night in Milton Keynes, where she hopes to wow her guests with her 'fabulous' menu - including bruschetta and mousse. However, the outspoken PA receives criticism from her guests - will she be able to overcome it to secure the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Management consultant David hosts the third dinner party in Milton Keynes, and his guests are impressed when he introduces some dancing Las Vegas showgirls. However, with all his focus on entertainment, David makes a major blunder with the food and serves vegetarian Helen a meat dish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Customer services manager and vegetarian Helen hosts the fourth night of the competition in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, and hopes that an East meets West-themed night will win her the £1,000 prize. Hypnotherapist Howard is intrigued by some items he finds in her bedroom, while salesman Simon enjoys her entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final evening in Milton Keynes and the dinner party is thrown by salesman Simon, who believes that his night of fine dining will impress the critical guests. However, things begin to go awry when Howard embarks on a mission to throw his plans off course, and Helen discovers a collection of awards that evidence the host's competitive nature. Meanwhile, Louise finally comes clean about her dislike of David, before the votes are cast and the winner is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B competition heads to France as four British expats compete, beginning with Lisanne Ashton, who runs the rural Les Chimeres in the south of the country. After searching for truffles in the surrounding area, guest Tony Horton's passion for the royal family causes him to clash with fellow competitor Shaun Baggs, whose mood is worsened by the discovery of bugs in his bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Le Manoir in the Dordogne, owned by royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou - who make a poor first impression by presenting guests with a timetable specifying when they are allowed to use the bathroom. A vinegar-tasting session also receives a tepid response, and some of the visitors are bemused by their hosts turning up for breakfast in costume

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wine-loving Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver bring the contest to Le Clos Rabelais in central France, where they invite fellow competitors to take part in a traditional French dance class. Tension mounts as George and Linda become increasingly frustrated with Lisanne, and the bickering continues over dinner when Shaun is confronted about his previous negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: George and Linda McGill welcome their guests to the Moulin de la Civiere near Rostrenen in Brittany, where entertainment comes in the shape of a tenpin-bowling outing, and the couple lay on a full English breakfast. However, the hosts receive an unexpected surprise at feedback time courtesy of Lisanne

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first in a week of parties in Norwich is hosted by hotel events manager Jo-Anne, who hopes a glamorous gourmet affair will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. As the five competitors take their places at Jo-Anne's dinner table, conversation takes an eclectic turn, covering everything from the Queen, Josie Gibson and Linda Robson to leopard-print pants and hovercraft

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Down-to-earth multimedia manager Karena hosts the second night in Norwich, where she hopes to impress her guests with some slightly more refined pub-style food, despite questionable table manners and hairy fish during the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former professional skier Jake hosts the third dinner party in Norwich, and hopes to impress his fellow contestants with a menu of fine dining and alpine classics. With the help of his mother, he decorates his house to create a winter wonderland, but the games he wants to play leave some guests feeling as if they are at a children's party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Music shop manager Barnaby takes the reins of the fourth night in Norwich, and after a fish-inspired starter and a main course of seafood linguine, his guests appear to be getting into the party spirit. However, it seems his only chance of scooping the prize money rests with his entertainment after ending up with a huge pile of leftovers - and a bleeding finger

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the night of the final dinner party in Norwich and the turn of business advisor Richard to host. He believes his Thai cooking will give him the edge in the competition, but has his work cut out as Jo-Anne is out to test his ego. Meanwhile, punk rocker Richard feels the spirit of rebellion and hopes to claim the cash prize for himself before the winner is announced

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw take on the restoration of one of the greatest British post-war sports cars ever built - a 1961 Austin Healey 3000

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on how South Yorkshire police tackle the issues of sexual offences, with officers called to a suspected sexual assault outside a nightclub, while in another early-morning incident, officers confront a man after he exposes himself in the street. Call handlers deal with a case of sexual assault on a teen by other teenagers, and a man is arrested on suspicion of domestic rape as a result of a call from his wife

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man has a blackout driving a van and crashes into a telegraph pole. This is the second time it has happened in as many weeks and paramedics are keen to find out why

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at St George's A&E in south-west London tend to 59-year-old Debby, who cut her hand on her lawnmower while distracted by thoughts of her son's forthcoming wedding. Also visiting the emergency room is 70-year-old George, whose eye has been impaled by a piece of driftwood, after he fell while bailing rainwater from his boat moored on the Thames. Plus, eight-year-old Phoebe arrives after accidentally sitting on a pair of scissors she left on a chair - getting them stuck in her thigh

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on how South Yorkshire police tackle the issues of sexual offences, with officers called to a suspected sexual assault outside a nightclub, while in another early-morning incident, officers confront a man after he exposes himself in the street. Call handlers deal with a case of sexual assault on a teen by other teenagers, and a man is arrested on suspicion of domestic rape as a result of a call from his wife

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man has a blackout driving a van and crashes into a telegraph pole. This is the second time it has happened in as many weeks and paramedics are keen to find out why

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The staff at St George's A&E in south-west London tend to 59-year-old Debby, who cut her hand on her lawnmower while distracted by thoughts of her son's forthcoming wedding. Also visiting the emergency room is 70-year-old George, whose eye has been impaled by a piece of driftwood, after he fell while bailing rainwater from his boat moored on the Thames. Plus, eight-year-old Phoebe arrives after accidentally sitting on a pair of scissors she left on a chair - getting them stuck in her thigh

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal allows himself to be led astray by rebellious new arrival Father Damien, and Ted is suspected of being the criminal mastermind behind a scandalous theft. Comedy, with Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Joe Rooney

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Amber and Colin turn their bedroom into a dream vintage haven. After an initial assessment, the presenter begins by getting rid of any mass-produced furniture

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: After a long-term relationship came to an end and her son flew the nest, Alix Sinclare sold her family home in Devon. She's now looking for the perfect place to start a new life but cannot decide between current home in Teignmouth, Devon, and Nerja on Spain's Costa del Sol. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin offer advice, with Jonnie anxious to convince her that Spain is the place to be

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon O'Brien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien finds a barn containing a collection of replica Rolls-Royce cars, and Henry Cole tries his hand at driving a tractor as they continue their money-making tour

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Return of the programme following the work of probate detectives as they try to find people who are unknowingly heirs, sometimes to substantial fortunes. In the first edition, the researchers unearth the history of Croydon's perfume industry as they trace the relatives of a woman who died in the seaside town of Poole, Dorset. Plus, two estranged brothers, who grew up unaware of the other's existence, meet for the first time

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following British expats as they chase a dream life in the sun and set up new businesses in Spain and France. Steve Stallion plans to open a bar on the Costa Blanca with his mother-in-law, a couple of total novices leave Scotland to run a B&B in the southwest of France, and fishing-boat charter boss Dean Hoyle hands over his beloved Costa del Sol business to a new owner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Ian and Sam Sugden's Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, where the guests are impressed with the cleanliness of the rooms. However, there is some grumbling over breakfast about the bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners stay at Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, which is run by Lisa Holloway - who prides herself on providing a home away from home for her visitors. However, some of the guests have issues with dust and the haddock served at breakfast - and challenge whether a night's stay is worth the price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Jo Bate welcome their guests to the White Horse, a pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, where they hope an afternoon of morris dancing in front of the cathedral will make a lasting impression. Unfortunately, Ed and Jane Mitchell get locked inside their room by mistake, and there are arguments over tomatoes at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell welcome the contestants to the Old Farmhouse, Cheshire, but everyone is shocked to discover they will have to share a bathroom. Meanwhile, Sam and Ian Sugden find an unwelcome surprise underneath their bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day arrives, as the guests gather to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences - from some fishy findings to a reminder of why sorry can be the hardest word. Later on, a heated exchange about cleaning ends in tears

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives find themselves under pressure to trace the relatives of a former RAF officer, but the task proves difficult because he has a common surname. Plus, the story of a man who, having already lost two brothers in the First World War, suffered further tragedy while protecting his family decades later during the Blitz

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Mr Parsons the chameleon meets a new partner, Daphne from Paris. Jaguars Napo and Goshi are being encouraged to mate, but Nap doesn't seem to be romantically inclined. Young giraffe Murchison faces the great outdoors for the first time, and another baby elephant is born, less than a month after the last new arrival

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge joins Elaine and David as they look around an 1850s chateau with 25 rooms, telling them what to look out for when buying such a vast property. The programme also catches up with Tim and Krys from the first series, who renovated rooms for a boutique B&B in the Dordogne, but have been struggling to juggle parenthood with running the business

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of musician Toby Leeming as he sets out to build a house wrapped around an ancient oak tree in rural Suffolk. The design for Toby's five-building home contains six bedrooms, six bathrooms, a music studio and an annexe - a vast project more like a village than a house. Creating this life changing vision on a £530k budget, and in only eight months, will not be an easy task, especially as they must build up close to an ancient oak without destroying any of the roots

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The stories behind sunken vessels around the world. The first episode features the exploration of two Scottish wrecks, HMS Hampshire and HMS Vanguard

programme.title: Hunt for the Arctic Ghost Ship
programme.desc: Cameras follow the multimillion-dollar expedition that discovered the Victorian ship HMS Erebus, intact and upright on the Arctic sea floor in September 2014. The story unfolds in the freezing Arctic, where search vessels battled with heavy sea-ice that threatened to stop the search entirely, to the dramatic chain of events that led to the historic discovery

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary charting the work of staff at St George's A&E continues, this time focusing on the stories of patients whose friendships have seen them through good times and bad. Phil, a 60-year-old bricklayer, is rushed in after suffering multiple injuries following a short fall from a ladder at work, and his business partner Paul discusses how the pair bonded over the course of 28 years. Meanwhile, 93-year-old widow Violet arrives after fracturing her leg while preparing to go out for lunch with the friends who have supported her since the death of her husband, and 18-year-old dance student Shiana comes to A&E with her mother and her best friend, after injuring her ankle during a rehearsal

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of the best bits from recent series with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley, joined by a host of top comedians including Alan Carr, Richard Ayoade, Kathy Burke, Roisin Conaty, Joe Wilkinson, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Ryan, Phil Wang, Aisling Bea and David Mitchell

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The stories behind sunken vessels around the world. The first episode features the exploration of two Scottish wrecks, HMS Hampshire and HMS Vanguard

programme.title: Hunt for the Arctic Ghost Ship
programme.desc: Cameras follow the multimillion-dollar expedition that discovered the Victorian ship HMS Erebus, intact and upright on the Arctic sea floor in September 2014. The story unfolds in the freezing Arctic, where search vessels battled with heavy sea-ice that threatened to stop the search entirely, to the dramatic chain of events that led to the historic discovery

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Chile to look into coloured crisps and find out how these rainbow snacks get their colour. Jimmy Doherty travels to Spain to investigate one of the trendiest items on the shelves, avocados, and the rapidly rising demand for these fruits which is leading to alarming reports of a crime wave hitting growers

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: The boys build a rink-making machine to host a charity hockey game, and attach two snow-blowers to the front of an ATV in order to clear a mountain of snow off the local lake

programme.title: Last Stop Garage
programme.desc: The boys build an ugly hot rod station wagon as a birthday present for a demanding client's wife

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Featuring a Texas attorney, who has a second life as a super picker, and possesses a wild warehouse of eclectic things

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef visits a five-star hotel in Madurai, Tamil Nadu, employing local housewives for genuine home-cooked dishes and samples chettinad chicken, a dish inspired by spices brought back from both East and West by merchants. In Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, he meets India's first MasterChef winner, and marks the end of his culinary journey with a small gathering of friends at his bungalow to reveal the outcome of his search for the perfect curry

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake journeys on some of the most perilous routes in the world. In Alaska, Sue Perkins and Charley Boorman drive along the notorious Dalton Highway, a dirt track originally constructed in the 1970s as a supply road for the Trans-Alaska oil pipeline. The pair struggle to cope with the Arctic conditions and constant threat of avalanches, but receive help from experienced truckers who teach them some unwritten rules for surviving the road's many hazards

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a race from London's Wembley Stadium to Milan's San Siro, the home of Italian football giants AC and Inter. A price restriction leads Jeremy to grab the keys to a Shelby Mustang GT500, which costs the equivalent of £35,000 in America and packs a supercharged punch of more than 650bhp. James and Richard pin their hopes on the pan-European rail network in a bid to secure the prize of tickets to a top-flight football match, with nothing but a long journey home awaiting the losers. On the test track, Jeremy puts Toyota's GT86 coupe through its paces, and its promise of entertaining rear-wheel-drive handling should put a smile on his face. Plus, singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald gets to grips with the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Kia Cee'd through a rigorous but peculiar road test featuring eels, curry, a rock legend, an American police officer and an unusual game of rugby involving James May. Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the Mastretta MXT, but worries about the repercussions of a comment he made in a previous episode about Mexico, the sports car's country of manufacture. Plus, three high-performance hatchbacks are driven with gusto around the test track, and Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton gets a second chance to smash the lap record

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: The Wieliczka Salt mine in southern Poland, some 327 metres deep, where the voids left behind from excavating salt have been transformed into extraordinary spaces, carved by hand

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: Documentary explaining how a squadron of low-flying bombers freed resistance fighters from Amiens Jail during the Second World War

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: First hand accounts of America's opening air raid on the Japanese archipelago, led by Colonel Jimmy Doolittle

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: Stories of people who came close to losing their lives during the Second World War, beginning with tales about the Chindits, the army's elite jungle troops division who beat the Japanese in a daring mission

programme.title: Russia from Above
programme.desc: Natural history series focusing on the spectacular scenery and indigenous wildlife of Russia, beginning with the nature living in and around Lake Manych-Gudilo

programme.title: Russia from Above
programme.desc: The indigenous wildlife of Russia's Asian South, featuring spectacular footage of the Volga delta, the shores of the Caspian Sea and Rostov-on-Don, towards the Black Sea

programme.title: Russia from Above
programme.desc: Focusing on the spectacular scenery and indigenous wildlife of Siberia, offering a bird's eye view of the world seldom seen before

programme.title: Russia from Above
programme.desc: The wildlife that thrives and survives in some of the more remote areas of Russia, including reindeer and walruses in places such as Cape Schmidt and Vankarem

programme.title: Russia from Above
programme.desc: The spectacular scenery and indigenous wildlife on Russia eastern coast, including volcanic eruptions and the gas and oli pipeline construction in the Amur River area

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Extended programme exploring the world's only sloth orphanage in Costa Rica, and meeting the characters that live there, including Alfie, who suffers from stunted growth

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Bohemia and Bavaria, beginning by exploring the art nouveau architecture of Czech Republic capital Prague. He then samples the sulphurous spa waters and wallows in peat and mud in Marianske Lazne, formerly known as Marienbad, before test driving a state-of-the-art passenger train made at the Skoda factory in Pilsen. Crossing the border into Germany, Michael encounters a fire-breathing dragon in Furth-im-Wald, and learns about an early 20th-century pioneer who laid the foundations for Munich's pre-eminence in science and technology

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Oakham in Rutland, where he learns about a noble tradition dating back to the Middle Ages and decides to take part. Heading east to Stamford, Lincolnshire, he discovers why the town is such an attractive location for period dramas, while a ghoulish scene awaits in Peterborough when he visits a Victorian operating theatre where railwaymen were treated. Michael's last stop on his journey is Christ's College at Cambridge University, where he finds out about the student days of Charles Darwin

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the country to discover how the railways have affected people and communities, and the legacy they left. The first leg of a journey through north-west England begins in Carlisle, where investigates the Victorian appetite for the custard cream, before braving a perilous descent into the only operational slate mine in England and discovering a miniature railway

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through the Lake District, where he discovers a magical world of talking rabbits, ducks, hedgehogs and mice, who have entertained children for more than 100 years. At the village home of author and illustrator Beatrix Potter, he learns about her legacy and her fears about the railways. Michael presses on to Brantwood, home of the Victorian art critic, John Ruskin, and finishes with a brief encounter at Carnforth

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reads the riot act in Preston, and discovers four mill workers were shot dead at a protest in 1842. In Darwen, he traces developments in interior design from wallpaper to paint, before exploring the Victorian industrial landscape of Salford in the paintings on LS Lowry. He finishes his leg of the journey on Kersal Moor, where he discovers the poetry of Edwin Waugh and twists his tongue around the Lancashire dialect

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from St Helens to Knutsford, finding out about modern glass-making and how techniques invented in the Victorian era to construct buildings such as the Crystal Palace have evolved and are powering a new architectural revolution

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey through north-west England, Michael Portillo makes a clean sweep in Ashley, Cheshire, where in Victorian times the new middle classes set up home in suburban villas with multiple chimneys swept by children. In Macclesfield, he finds the end of the Silk Route and tries his hand at screen printing. Then after stoking the fire on the steam-powered Churnet Valley Railway, the former politician alights at Froghall, Staffordshire, heading for Alton Towers to trace the 19th-century origins of the theme park

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Godber takes up boxing and enters the prison championships. He is hot favourite to win until Fletcher is given the task of fixing the contest and nobbling his cell-mate. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Peter Vaughan

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Harris accidentally drops stolen pills into Fletcher's midday cuppa - much to the annoyance of prison racketeer Harry Grout, who resents a newcomer muscling in on his patch. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher objects to having a third cellmate foisted on him, especially as the newcomer seems to enjoy special privileges and the full backing of the wardens. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay

programme.title: The Rolling Stones at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of the band's performances from the BBC archives, in a programme originally broadcast in 2012 to celebrate their 50th anniversary. Songs include The Last Time, Let's Spend The Night Together, Get Off of My Cloud, Honky Tonk Women, Gimme Shelter, Miss You and Start Me Up. Plus, footage of the band performing 19th Nervous Breakdown on Top of the Pops in 1966, which was used in a 1960s BBC documentary about women with depression

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of the Tokyo Skytree, the world's tallest free-standing tower, which uses liquid springs and safety glass to withstand earthquakes and high winds

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A fully relocatable polar research station in Antarctica, which was inspired by a revolutionary icebreaking ship and ingenious designs for pre-fabricated buildings

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: A labyrinth of tunnels underneath a tiny French village which contains mysteries from several historical eras, and a Welsh slate mine that has been given a new lease of life

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: An underground city beneath a forest in Northern France built to house 3,000 people, an ice cave under Iceland's biggest glacier, and a subterranean power station in London

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. In the opening episode, they restore a Grenadier guard's drum, create a garden feature from a helicopter tripod and re-purpose a vintage car grille into a gleaming design classic

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis re-invents an old ships compass and a grand display cabinet into two of his most successful transformations to date

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis visits an automobilia auction at Silverstone, creates a chair out of Second World War bomber jackets and gives a Victorian table a new zinc top

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Extended programme exploring the world's only sloth orphanage in Costa Rica, and meeting the characters that live there, including Alfie, who suffers from stunted growth

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the vast and diverse continent, aiming to show its landscapes and wildlife as they have never been seen before. He begins in the Kalahari desert, Africa's south-west corner, where clever meerkats are outsmarted by a wily bird's use of mimicry, a solitary rhino is seen to exhibit previously unknown sociable behaviours, terrifying insects prey on baby birds and the rarest fish in the world is filmed for the first time. In the neighbouring Namib desert, which receives almost no rain, the competition for scant resources can be fierce, as proven by a ferocious fight for supremacy between two giraffes

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough continues his exploration of the continent in east Africa, which is a haven for life and supports a higher density of large mammals than anywhere else on Earth. From dense forests to snow-capped peaks, steamy swamps to endless savannah, it's also a land of unpredictable extremes, where creatures must adapt to survive. In Tanzania, a tiny lizard risks its life to eat flies from the face of a sleepy lion. The nesting habits of the 4ft shoebill are also captured on camera - the giant bird has two chicks, but due to sibling rivalry only one will survive. On the plains of Amboseli, a baby elephant is near to death in the drought, so its mother leaves the herd to stay with her offspring until the bitter end

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the varied creatures of the Congo rainforest, one of the most competitive habitats on Earth. A sweet-toothed chimpanzee uses a complex array of tools to access honey from a beehive, the elusive picathartes bird feeds unsuspecting frogs to its dinosaur-like chicks and a massive female python reveals her maternal side as she sunbathes to incubate her eggs. Tiny leaf-folding frogs battle it out kung fu style, and as darkness falls the forest elephants gather to face their rivals

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes some of the wildlife spectacles of southern Africa, starting out in a little-explored rainforest discovered less than a decade ago by scientists who spotted it on satellite maps. There, thousands of butterflies dance for the right to mate, while on a tiny island off Mozambique, green turtles emerge from eggs and begin a hazardous journey to the water - with pied crows and hungry crabs eager to get them. Cameras also capture one of the largest gatherings of great white sharks on the planet as 30 of the predators circle around a dead whale

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the wildlife eking out an existence in and around the scorching wilderness of the Sahara, where only the toughest survive. It's a constant struggle against dehydration, as zebra battle over dwindling rivers, camels search for water and tiny barn swallows navigate thousands of square miles of barren sand to find a life-saving oasis. The naked mole rat avoids the heat by living a subterranean existence, while the silver ant is able to take on the midday sun - but needs a 'spacesuit' to do it

programme.title: Attenborough's Living Lights
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the world of bioluminescence, the often spectacular natural light produced by creatures including glow-worms, fireflies and plankton, using specially designed cameras, some fitted to a new generation of deep-sea submersibles and robots to record instances in the very deep ocean. The programme shows a millipede glowing on the forest floor in California, the mesmerising flashing light patterns of the synchronous firefly, the spooky glow of a rare dragon fish and luminous earth worms in France

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Von Klinkerhoffen's trust for the Gestapo is rapidly diminishing, so he orders Helga to marry Herr Flick so she can keep an eye on his activities. Elsewhere, the British airmen head to the canal to rendezvous with the submarine waiting for them. Comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Hilary Minster and Kim Hartman

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene and his staff disguise themselves as undertakers in a bid to rescue the British airmen. Meanwhile, Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen decide they want the pilots to join the Gestapo and dress up as German officers - but are arrested before they get chance to put their plan into action

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle concocts another plan to infiltrate German headquarters and rescue the British airmen using hijacked German uniforms, but who will they fit?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Three rare Alvises get the auction buyers going when put under the hammer. A Chevy Corvette which started its journey in Saudi Arabia makes its way to Thornton le Dale for the sale

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction and maintenance of the International Space Station, revealing the influence of an unconventional aircraft designer and a 19th-century rebreather

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Underground Worlds
programme.desc: The underground bases built by the German army during the occupation of Jersey, and a group of more than 20 underground cities in Turkey, one of which dates back to 800BC

programme.title: Underground Worlds

programme.title: Private Lives of the Monarchs
programme.desc: Tracy Borman explores the decadent Reformation court of Charles II, and uncovers some little-known facts about the king's famous mistress Nell Gwynn

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the Château de Fontainebleau in France, which began life as a medieval castle in the 12th century before being turned into a renaissance palace in the 1500s

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: A visit to Peles Castle in Romania, which was built in a medieval style in 1914 but is filled with modern technological innovations, including its own hydro-electric power plant

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Bohemia and Bavaria, beginning by exploring the art nouveau architecture of Czech Republic capital Prague. He then samples the sulphurous spa waters and wallows in peat and mud in Marianske Lazne, formerly known as Marienbad, before test driving a state-of-the-art passenger train made at the Skoda factory in Pilsen. Crossing the border into Germany, Michael encounters a fire-breathing dragon in Furth-im-Wald, and learns about an early 20th-century pioneer who laid the foundations for Munich's pre-eminence in science and technology

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Burton Constable Hall near Hull, which is home to a collection of treasures. Objects brought in to be valued include a ship's anchor that was found in a garden pond, a medal awarded to sailor for helping to recover Captain Scott's ship from the Antarctic in 1901, some 17th-century flat pack furniture, and a flimsy booklet that proved to be a profitable investment

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant returns for more train journeys through some of the world's most challenging terrain. He heads to the Arctic for a trip along the Alaska Railroad, but as he arrives in the port of Seward, it's not a great start as there are no trains running, so he ends up hitching a ride with a track inspector's car. He continues his journey on a freight train heading for Anchorage, Alaska's biggest city, and finally heads north on the Aurora passenger train to the town of Healy. Along the way he checks out the tiny village of Eklutna, which has great importance for the indigenous inhabitants, and goes for an intrepid fishing trip on a thawing frozen lake

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A small isolated country littered with strange concrete mushrooms and a secret Japanese facility that was once at the forefront of weapons technology

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An impressive compound commandeered and repurposed by a brutal regime and an eerie complex off the coast of Ireland that became the biggest of its kind

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: The life of Josef Mengele, the notorious demon doctor of Auschwitz, who conducted barbaric experiments on innocent victims - including children - in the concentration camp

programme.title: Mission: Kill Hitler
programme.desc: A plot to kill the fuhrer that remained secret until the late 1990s. Operation Foxley planned to murder Hitler during his daily morning walk at the Berghof, his Bavarian residence. Two soldiers parachuted into the area were supposed to find a perfect location to shoot Hitler and stop the devastating war. Based on the British Secret Services' declassified documents, using archives and fictionalised reconstitutions, this film recounts the full story and the detailed plan that should have led to the murder of Adolf Hitler, and explains why it failed.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Burton Constable Hall near Hull, which is home to a collection of treasures. programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB host a 90th birthday party, but the cooking proves challenging and JB has a near disaster with his boar roast. May and her family start work on turning a rundown outbuilding into a yoga retreat. For Tim and Margreeth, it's the day of the wedding party, but the venue - the shed in the woods - isn't quite finished. With hungry guests waiting to be fed, the bride and groom are getting increasingly anxious

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Geri Melville is originally from St Lucia, but moved to the UK when she was just nine. Now, she's ready to return to her roots with her husband Ian, by buying a holiday home together on the island she left almost 50 years ago. With a budget of £360,000 Laura Hamilton shows them five properties boasting views of the Caribbean Sea

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Warren and Jacqui Sumner from Manchester first visited Crete on their honeymoon 23 years ago and have been going back ever since. They've fallen in love with the island and are now determined to buy their very own holiday home there. Danni Menzies shows them five properties for their £75,000 budget, but will any of them be the romantic getaway they've been dreaming of?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Marianne and Paul, who want a holiday home on a golf resort around the stunning Mar Menor in Murcia, Spain, on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bernie and Joy from Liverpool are determined to buy a property in Cyprus, so Danni Menzies shows them five properties in the east of the island for their £35,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas offers advice to Karen and Paul from Dundee, who are looking for a holiday home on Spain's stunning Mijas Costa where they can enjoy family getaways with their teenage son Nicholas. They would love to find an apartment with lots of outside space

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in the Scottish capital. Zoe and Jason are returning to their native Edinburgh after a decade living and working in Hong Kong. They have £950,000 but that will only buy them a flat around their preferred area. Can Phil fulfil their expensive wishlist? Kirstie is with Tan, from Lisbon, and his Fife-born wife Stephanie, who cannot agree on a countryside feel or city vibe

programme.title: Guy Martin's Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter experiences the training of a Second World War fighter pilot, ultimately taking to the skies in a Hawker Hurricane for a dogfight against a Messerschmitt 109. In the first part, he learns how to fly in a Tiger Moth bi-plane, before progressing to the much faster Harvard aircraft and combat manoeuvres. He also tries his hand at clay pigeon shooting, which was used as a training exercise to help rookie pilots shoot fast-moving targets

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show with a difference, in which another two singletons take their pick of potential partners based purely on their naked bodies. First up is 27-year-old Kelly, a carer from Brighton. She's unsure whether she's looking for a man or a woman but hopes to find her the perfect partner to give her a little TLC. She is followed by 29-year-old steeplejack Carlo from Somerset, who is looking for woman that he can sweep off her feet

programme.title: Second Hand for 50 Grand
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a company that tracks down luxury second-hand items, going the extra mile to hunt down the exact handbags, watches and jewellery on their clients' wish-lists and find out the personal stories behind them. Window cleaner Grant's dad wants to buy him a Cartier watch as a thank you for looking after him. Rebecca asks head of handbags Reece to scour the globe for an exact match to a stolen Prada bag gifted by her late mother. Meanwhile, firefighter Hywell has received a watch from his Grandad. If the team can prove that it's genuine, then Hywell's life is about to change for ever

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on recently aired shows, including watching Game of Talents, Line of Duty, Catfish UK: The TV Show, Four in a Bed, Paranormal Activity and Cher & the Loneliest Elephant. Plus, they share their observations of the coverage of the funeral of HRH the Duke of Edinburgh and Good Morning Britain's report of an altercation between Labour leader Keir Starmer and a pub landlord

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Paul Foot take on Jon Richardson and Angela Barnes in the words-and-numbers game, while poet John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Lonesome teenager Jasmine seeks a canine companion to replace her twin sister Shola, who's leaving her to start a university course. Her family thinks that pedigree beagle Zoe will make the perfect new sibling, but Jasmine isn't so sure. Elsewhere, staff of Wood Green animal charity believe Jim the poodle could prove the perfect match for 12-year-old Lexi, who is looking for a 'bedtime buddy'

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates how to make an avocado pastry quiche, which uses avocado in the pastry instead of butter or oil. He also offers some tips on how to make a barbecue extra special, and explains some new and inventive ways to cook a juicy seared steak

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits India on the trail of turmeric and finds himself at a wedding ceremony where guests are showered with the exotic spice. Kate Quilton finds out how much milk goes into a bar of chocolate, while Andi Oliver uncovers the reason why asparagus delivers some of its eaters with very pungent urine

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition commences at Churchill's Hotel in Barnsley, where the guests are treated to a day out at a nearby mill and the hotel's first ever breakfast service

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at Darnells' Farm Luxury Glamping in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, where arachnophobe Snehal checks every corner of his lodging for unwanted eight-legged guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners stay the night at Hatton Court Hotel in Gloucester, a country style establishment that at first proves a hit - until cleanliness concerns, sleepless nights and missing breakfast items leave them disappointed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to the Ladbrooke Hotel in the heart of Birmingham, where manager Snehal Lakhani claims he accepts nothing less than perfection. A room with a broken window proves far from flawless for Natasha and Laura, while Danni and Graham are concerned about the proximity of a train line to their bedroom

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, Churchill's Hotel's Jeff Brown and Vicky O'Loughlin are first to face the feedback and Jeff's keen to turn down the heating woes. Plus, Snehal from the Ladbrooke Hotel has a bone to pick with all of the B&Bers. After some frank discussions, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: When Erin and JB can't fix the doors back onto their solarium, Dick Strawbridge steps in to help them find a solution to their rusted hinges. Another leak is found, but this time from the roof, so they call in some professional climbers to scale their bell tower to find the source of the problem. With wedding season over, Tim and Margreeth launch a foodie weekend. With a professional chef hired to produce the food and some trainee butlers on hand to serve more than 20 guests, the couple can take it easy - apart from foraging for mushrooms, dressing the dining room and fixing a guest's belt. Meanwhile, May and her son Jack get the yoga room ready for the imminent arrival of guests.

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB launch their chateau business by opening it up for a tea party for the locals, including the mayor. May holds her health and wellbeing retreat using her new yoga studio for the first time

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Brooklyn, New York, to tackle a 55-year-old family business. Owner John Esposito left school at the age of 14 and has worked at his parents' restaurant, Sal's Pizzeria, ever since. Their success meant they could afford to purchase the location next door in the 1990s, naming it Mama Maria after John's mother, who died in 2002. But when he lost his father as well, John became overwhelmed by the responsibility of running both establishments and his livelihood is at risk

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Wilkinsburg in Pennsylvania, home to Ms Jean's Southern Cuisine, which was opened by retired special-education teacher Jean Gould. During the chef's attempt to save the business from disaster, he discovers her soul-food restaurant resembles a dentist's waiting room and finds a lot of tension between Jean and her employees, who claim she is a Jekyll-and-Hyde character

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies is house-hunting in the foothills of the Sierra Nevada mountain range with keen adventurer Mark, who wants to relocate to the Spanish countryside

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Hyacinth Miller from London first visited Barbados 14 years ago, and now that she's retired, wants to permanently relocate there and spend time in the garden. With her son Leon along to help choose the right property, Jasmine Harman shows Hyacinth five properties in the south of the island, but do any of them have her packing her bags for a new life in the sun?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonathan and Stephen have busy lives running their own businesses, and can't wait to buy a holiday home near the glitzy town of Marbella on Spain's Costa del Sol. The couple are looking for an apartment near the beach, where they can chill out with friends while enjoying spectacular sea views on Spain's sunshine coast. With a budget of £250,000 Jean Johansson rises to the challenge, presenting the pair with five property options to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: James first visited Crete over 40 years ago and has always longed to buy a holiday home there. Now that his partner Alison has also fallen in love with the Greek island too, they've both decided to turn their dream into a reality. Ben Hillman helps the couple find a suitable holiday home - ideally a traditional village house - on their budget of £80,000

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect heads off to Italy, where it seems the residents have also been bitten by the 'small buildings are beautiful' bug. The most spectacular place he visits is so high up in the mountains that it's only accessible by helicopter, but this difficulty is more than made up for by the views seen from it. Back in Britain, George meets a man turning a wrecked private jet into a crash pad and a Gypsy tattoo artist with big plans for an old railway carriage. George also challenges his pal, craftsman Will Hardie, to create a woodland retreat for less than the price of a family holiday

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: After a four year stint living in New Zealand, ex-RAF pilot Jon and GP Gill Flewers want to build a Kiwi-style hill house on the slopes of the Malvern hills in Worcestershire. They plan to build an ambitious three-storey home clad in wood and stone, but the pair encounter problems as the site is so steep builders do not want to take on the job

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Paul Foot take on Jon Richardson and Angela Barnes in the words-and-numbers game, while poet John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Guy Martin's Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Part two of two. Guy's training ramps up at the UK's only aerial combat school, where he must master dogfighting at 200mph, handling extreme g-force, and flying upside down. He enlists the help of the Parachute Regiment for a unique experiment demonstrating the firepower of Second World War aircraft machine guns, before being allowed behind the joystick of the world's only two-seater Hawker Hurricane to face a Messerschmitt 109 in the skies over Kent

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show with a difference, in which another two singletons take their pick of potential partners based purely on their naked bodies. programme.desc: Architecture student Priti has trouble finding a man in the academic world and wants someone older, rugged and tattooed. No stranger to the tattooist's chair is Australian entrepreneur Nick - but will the age gap end up being a problem for these two singletons? Firefighter Katie and her date - also called Katie - open up about significant turning points in their lives, while superhero fanatic Richard thinks he has hit the jackpot when his date turns out to be a self-confessed geek and Comic Con fan
